1146880.failure_event: Single phase: 9.9kW(6.6kW*1.5)450us three phase: 16.5kW(11kW*1.5)450us when the above condition is met, stops OBC and count wait for 100ms then restart. If the count >5 times?Faulure trigger. or DC side input power > 9.6kW(6kW*1.6)7.5ms when the above condition is met, stops OBC and count wait for 100ms then restart. If the count >5 times?Faulure trigger.

1474596.description: The MCU_R inlet water temperature sensor fault
1474596.cause: 1. The sensor is damaged or the wiring harness is open; 2. The sensor or cable harness is short-circuited.
1474596.impact: affect the three-way valve, condensing fan, active grille, water pump control
1474596.limp: 1. Send the fault code; 2. Fixed opening of three-way valve, fixed speed of condensing fan, maximum opening of active grille, maximum output of water pump;
1474596.repair: Replace the sensor or Check the wiring harness
1474596.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age The temperature of the temperature sensor exceeds the range of -40! ~80! or exceeds the temperature range marked by the sensor RT table for 2s The temperature of the sensor outside the vehicle returned to the range of -40! ~80! , and returned to the temperature range marked by RT table, lasting for 2s 40 times driving cycle

1474615.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for temp return to normal
1474615.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age EXV_B_FltSta == Over Temp shutdown EXV_B_FltSta == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474616.impact: The flow openness of the cooling loop of the system is affected, and the cooling capability of the battery is insufficient or its functions are unavailable
1474616.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age EXV_B_FltSta == Motor-Coil shorted or Motor-Coil open load EXV_B_FltSta == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474621.impact: The three-way valve can not be adjusted, resulting in the power cooling loop front drive loop or rear drive loop component temperature rise, affecting the vehicle power output
1474621.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for LIN return to normal
1474621.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous LIN Message period Trigger Reset Age No CVM message is received for 10 consecutive message periods or CVM response CVM_ RESP_ERROR == error CVM LIN message received and C VM_RESP_ER ROR == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474622.cause: input voltage of the CVM is too high or too low
1474622.impact: The water valve does not work, affecting the cooling effect of the driving motor
1474622.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for Volt return to normal
1474622.repair: Check the input voltage of the CVM
1474622.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age CVM_OverVolta geFlt == fail or CVM_UnderVolt ageFlt == fail CVM_OverVolta geFlt == normal and CV M_UnderVoltag eFlt == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474623.impact: The water valve does not work, affecting the cooling effect of the driving motor
1474623.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for current return to normal
1474623.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age CVM_OverCurr ent == fail CVM_OverCurr ent == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474624.impact: The water valve does not work, affecting the cooling effect of the driving motor
1474624.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for temp return to normal
1474624.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age CVM_OverTem pFlt == fail CVM_OverTem pFlt == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474625.cause: The LIN bus is faulty or Component failure
1474625.impact: Power system can not cool, passenger compartment and battery pack can not actively cool, affecting the vehicle power output and charging
1474625.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for LIN return to normal
1474625.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous LIN Message period Trigger Reset Age No AGS message is received for 10 consecutive message periods or AGS response AGS_ RESP_ERROR == error AGS LIN message received and A GS_RESP_ER ROR == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474626.impact: Power system can not cool, passenger compartment and battery pack can not actively cool, affecting the vehicle power output and charging
1474626.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for return to normal
1474626.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age AGS_OverTrave Flt == fail AGS_OverTrave Flt == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474627.impact: Power system can not cool, passenger compartment and battery pack can not actively cool, affecting the vehicle power output and charging
1474627.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for return to normal
1474627.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age AGS_Blockage Flt == fail AGS_Blockage Flt == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474628.impact: Power system can not cool, passenger compartment and battery pack can not actively cool, affecting the vehicle power output and charging
1474628.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for return to normal
1474628.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age AGS_Electrical Flt == fail AGS_Electrical Flt == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474629.cause: Undervoltage or Overvoltage fault of the active grille
1474629.impact: Power system can not cool, passenger compartment and battery pack can not actively cool, affecting the vehicle power output and charging
1474629.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for Volt return to normal
1474629.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age AGS_UnderVolt Flt == fail or AG S_OverVoltFlt == fail AGS_UnderVolt Flt == normal and AGS_Over VoltFlt == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474630.cause: Over temperature fault of active grille
1474630.impact: Power system can not cool, passenger compartment and battery pack can not actively cool, affecting the vehicle power output and charging
1474630.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for temperature return to normal
1474630.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age AGS_OverTemp Flt == fail AGS_OverTemp Flt == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474631.cause: The LIN bus is faulty or Component failure
1474631.impact: The battery thermal management function is lost, affecting the range and charging speed;
1474631.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for LIN return to normal
1474631.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous LIN Message period Trigger Reset Age No EWP message is received for 10 consecutive message periods or EXV response EWP_ B_RESP_ERR OR == error EWP LIN message received and E WP_B_RESP_ ERROR == normal 40 times driving cycle

1474632.cause: Lack of coolant or There are a lot of bubbles in the coolant
1474632.impact: The water pump has detected a dry transfer stop, affecting the cooling effect
1474632.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for EWP return to normal
1474632.repair: Add coolant and Whether there is leakage in the pipeline
1474632.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age EWP_B_DryRu nWarn == dry run EWP_B_DryRu nWarn == no dry run 40 times driving cycle

1474633.cause: 1. The impeller is jammed by a foreign object; 2. The pump speed is too low; 3.Viscous coolant (low temperature, less likely, depending on the coolant used)
1474633.impact: The water pump will stop due to the detection of blocked rotation, affecting the cooling effect
1474633.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for EWP return to normal
1474633.repair: Check whether the pump impeller is stuck
1474633.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age EWP_B_Block Warn == stall EWP_B_Block Warn == no stall 40 times driving cycle

1474634.cause: 1. If the current of the pump is too high, the pump is overloaded or the pump is abnormal. 2. If the current is too high when the pump stops, the ECU inside the pump is damaged.
1474634.impact: The pump stopped and the battery could not be cooled or heated
1474634.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for EWP return to normal
1474634.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age EWP_B_OverC urrWarn == Over current EWP_B_OverC urrWarn == No Over current 40 times driving cycle

1474635.description: EWP_B over or under voltage fault
1474635.cause: 1.The vehicle battery is fed, and the voltage is less than 8V. 2.The vehicle battery voltage exceeds 16V.
1474635.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.The battery loop stops working
1474635.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age EWP_B_SupVol tWarn == Over voltage or EWP _B_SupVoltWar n == Under voltage EWP_B_SupVol tWarn == Normal voltage 40 times driving cycle

1474636.cause: The ambient temperature of the pump or coolant is too high
1474636.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.The battery loop stops working
1474636.repair: Check whether the pump works in an overheated environment. If not, replace the pump.
1474636.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age EWP_B_OverTe mpWarn == Over temperature EWP_B_OverTe mpWarn == No over temperature 40 times driving cycle

1474639.cause: Lack of coolant or There are a lot of bubbles in the coolant
1474639.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for EWP return to normal
1474639.repair: Add coolant and Whether there is leakage in the pipeline
1474639.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age EWP_H_DryRu nWarn == dry run EWP_H_DryRu nWarn == no dry run 40 times driving cycle

1474640.cause: 1. The impeller is jammed by a foreign object; 2. The pump speed is too low; 3.Viscous coolant (low temperature, less likely, depending on the coolant used)
1474640.limp: 1.Send the fault code; 2.wait for EWP return to normal
1474640.repair: Check whether the pump impeller is stuck
1474640.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Ignition ON or ECC is awake Continuous 100ms Trigger Reset Age EWP_H_Block Warn == stall EWP_H_Block Warn == no stall 40 times driving cycle

14221924.cause: Potentiometer resistance is out of valid range.
14221924.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check if the potentiometer value is changing while the motor is moving (mirror must be moving). Measure the resistance of the potentiometer and check if it is in the specified range. In case that the resistance value isn't changing or the value is out of range, replace the mirror.
14221924.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Potentiometer resistance is out of valid range. Potentiometer resistance is within valid range. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14222181.cause: Potentiometer resistance is out of valid range.
14222181.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check if the potentiometer value is changing while the motor is moving (mirror must be moving). Measure the resistance of the potentiometer and check if it is in the specified range. In case that the resistance value isn't changing or the value is out of range, replace the mirror.
14222181.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Potentiometer resistance is out of valid range. Potentiometer resistance is within valid range. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

1049671.subsystem: This DTC indicates that the SW safety functions within L3 are not called in the correct order or cylce. The program flow monitoring checks the control flow. In case of a mismatch (not executed, wrong order...) the WD refresh is intentionally wrong and the ECU performs a reset.
1049671.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. If ignition cycle change is not performed in 10 minutes VCU will shutdown itself. Vehicle is only able to roll out. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1049671.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues
1049671.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1049671.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

1049672.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues

14222438.description: Left Mirror Motor X - Motor stalled
14222438.cause: Mirror position hasn't changed although motor is driven and hasn't hit the hardstop.
14222438.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check if the potentiometer value is changing while the motor is moving (mirror must be moving). Measure the resistance of the potentiometer and check if it is in the specified range. In case that the resistance value isn't changing or the value is out of range, replace the mirror.
14222438.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Mirror position hasn't changed although motor is driven and hasn't hit the hardstop. Mirror is moving while driven. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14222695.description: Left Mirror Motor Y - Motor stalled
14222695.cause: Mirror position hasn't changed although motor is driven and hasn't hit the hardstop.
14222695.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check if the potentiometer value is changing while the motor is moving (mirror must be moving). Measure the resistance of the potentiometer and check if it is in the specified range. In case that the resistance value isn't changing or the value is out of range, replace the mirror.
14222695.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Mirror position hasn't changed although motor is driven and hasn't hit the hardstop. Mirror is moving while driven. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

6587811.failure_event: This failure is only used while development. It handles a failed assertion according the ANSI C standard. 14222952.description: Right Mirror Motor X - Motor stalled
14222952.cause: Mirror position hasn't changed although motor is driven and hasn't hit the hardstop.
14222952.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check if the potentiometer value is changing while the motor is moving (mirror must be moving). Measure the resistance of the potentiometer and check if it is in the specified range. In case that the resistance value isn't changing or the value is out of range, replace the mirror.
14222952.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Mirror position hasn't changed although motor is driven and hasn't hit the hardstop. Mirror is moving while driven. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

6588064.description: Clockin monitor of system IC reports wrong clockin failure status to uC
6588064.subsystem: Erroneous safety logic (The clockin monitor of the system IC reports wrong clockin failure status to the uC)
6588064.failure_event: The failsafe logic test tests whether the clockin monitor of the system IC detects a too high, a too low or a correct clockin frequency. This failure is logged if the feedback to the uC does not show the expected clockin failure state.
6588064.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6588064.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6588066.description: Missing BIST trigger didn't swicth off hy. or electrically as expected
6588066.subsystem: Erroneous safety logic (e.g. BIST of system IC either does not detect a correct BIST command or missing BIST commands)
6588066.failure_event: The failsafe logic test tests whether the watchdog of the system chip can be started with the first watchdog trigger signal from the BIST state machine. Additionally it is tested that the watchdog switches off hydraulically respective electrically if the BIST state machine is not triggered during the corresponding timeout times.
6588066.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6588066.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6588067.description: BIST didn't switch off electrically after too much received wrong BIST commands
6588067.subsystem: Erroneous safety logic (BIST of system IC does not switch off electrically after too much received wrong BIST commands)
6588067.failure_event: The failsafe logic test tests that after 3 wrong BIST commands the BIST failure counter counted up to its max. value and that the BIST state machine switches off electrically consequently.
6588067.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6588067.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

14223209.description: Right Mirror Motor Y - Motor stalled
14223209.cause: Mirror position hasn't changed although motor is driven and hasn't hit the hardstop.
14223209.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check if the potentiometer value is changing while the motor is moving (mirror must be moving). Measure the resistance of the potentiometer and check if it is in the specified range. In case that the resistance value isn't changing or the value is out of range, replace the mirror.
14223209.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Mirror position hasn't changed although motor is driven and hasn't hit the hardstop. Mirror is moving while driven. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

1050884.subsystem: This DTC indicates latents fault detected during startup of the Systembasis Chip which could lead to the unavailability of its safety functions. a.) The Logic of the Fail Safe State Machine is covered by the check "LBIST". In case of a latent fault the Fail Safe State Machine or Question / Answer Game Watchdog is not able to trigger the Fail Safe Outputs. b.) The Fail Safe Outputs (RSTb, FS0b) and the Voltage Supervisor (VPRE, VCORE, VCCA) are covered by the check "ABIST1". In case of a latent fault, the Fail Safe Output would not be able to enter the safe state or the Voltage Supervisors are not able to detect a "out of range over / under voltage condition" c.) The Voltage Supervisor (VAUX) is covered by the check "ABIST2". In case of an latent fault the Voltage Supervisor is not able to detect "out of range over / under voltage conditions" or an "over- / undervoltage condition is already present" which prevents the fail safe output to be released.
1050884.failure_event: Latent fault detected by BIST during startup
1050884.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. If ignition cycle change is not performed in 10 minutes VCU will shutdown itself. Vehicle is only able to roll out. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1050884.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues
1050884.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG

1050902.subsystem: This DTC indicates a under voltage condition of the internal voltage (VPRE) which is acts as a pre regulator for the voltage regulators for Core supply . Since the voltage is below the operating conditions of the System Basis Chip itself, the cascaded voltage regulator VCORE will result in a undervoltage condition.
1050902.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. If ignition cycle change is not performed in 10 minutes VCU will shutdown itself. Vehicle is only able to roll out. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1050902.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues
1050902.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1050902.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

1050966.subsystem: This DTC indicates a wrong HW configuration of the Fail Safe features of the Systembasis Chip which results in an unexpected behavior.ims to detect and react on latent faults in the HW a.) The Debug Mode - HW Input of the Fail Safe State Machine is covered by the check: DBG_HW. In Debug Mode - the Fail Safe State Machine doesn't assert the Reset output in case of the Watchdog is not serviced properly and in addition the Fail Safe Output can not be released. b.) The Deep Reset & Fail Configuration is covered by the check DFS_HW1 and DFS_HW2. In case of the Deep Reset & Fail Feature is not active, the device would not "turn Off" all voltage regulators in case of a serious HW failure.
1050966.failure_event: Debug Mode check fails or Deep Sleep & Fail Configuration check fails
1050966.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. If ignition cycle change is not performed in 10 minutes VCU will shutdown itself. Vehicle is only able to roll out. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1050966.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues
1050966.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1050966.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

1051041.subsystem: This DTC indicates a wrong HW configuration of the Voltage Regulators of the Systembasis Chip which can cause single point faults. a.) VCCA_PNP configuration covers the current capability and the accuracy of the 5V reference for the ADC of the microcontroller. A failure causes an increased inaccuracy of ADC measurement. b.) VCCA_HW configuration covers the supply voltage level of the 5V reference for the ADC of the Microcontroller. A failure causes an corrupted ADC measurement. c.) VAUX_HW configuration covers the supply voltage level of analog peripherals (e.g. current sensors, rotor position sensor). A failure causes an corrupted analog measurement. d.) LS_DETECT configuration covers the Buck / Boost Configuration of the pre - regulator. A wrong configuration causes a limited functionality at under - voltage conditions. SBC_VPRE_CONFIG specifies the intended HW population (0... Buck only, 1.. Buck Boost)
1051041.failure_event: 6V5Pre Configuration Check or VCCA VAUX configuration check fails
1051041.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. If ignition cycle change is not performed in 10 minutes VCU will shutdown itself. Vehicle is only able to roll out. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1051041.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU
1051041.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG

14221410.cause: Potentiometer resistance is out of valid range.
14221410.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (mirror must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is above the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14221410.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Potentiometer resistance is out of valid range. Potentiometer resistance is within valid range. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

1051121.subsystem: This DTC indicates a latent fault detected during runtime of the Systembasis Chip which could lead to the unavailability of its safety functions. a.) The Majority Voter of the triplicated structure of the Fault Error Counter of the Fail Safe State Machine is covered by the check: ERR_INT_HW b.) Internal trim registers of the clock of the Fail Safe State Machine and the Bandgap Reference of the Voltage Supervisor are covered by the check ERR_INT_SW

1051121.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1051121.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues

1051131.subsystem: This DTC indicates a failiure in the connection of the Reset between Microcontroller and Systembasis Chip. In case of an open connection or short to high the SBC would not be able to reset the Microcontroller which is considered as latent fault. In case of the short to low the Microcontroller is a permanent reset state and is not able to communicate to the outside world via Bus. A DTC is not possible, but the ECU enters the deep reset and fail and turns off all internal regulators.

1231360.description: DC-DC Converter input available power under threshold
1231360.subsystem: 1. The solar panel is abnormal 2. In the absence of sunlight 3. Input voltage detection circuit abnormal 4. Input current detection circuit abnormal
1231360.possible_causes: 1. Disable output, and try to recovery after 3s 2. Report the fault status and fault code 3. Record the DTC
1231360.impact: 1. Disable output, and try to recovery after 3s 2. Report the fault status and fault code 3. Record the DTC
1231360.steps: 1. Check the voltage of solar panel 2. Restart, if the DTC still exist, then replace the DC-DC
1231360.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1231360.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).
1231360.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. The 12V battery voltage above 9V and below 18V AND 2. DC-DC converter works in the state of charging Continuous 5ms Trigger Reset Age InputPowerAvail <40W,t>10min Auto-cleared after DC-DC converter disable output 40dec

1051137.subsystem: This DTC indicates a failure detected by the Power Management - Monitoring Build In Self Test of the Microcontoller. After reset release, "PMS:MONBIST" for the "secondary voltage monitors" and "alarm generation path" is carried out by SafeCore L3 SW. Secondary voltage monitor Build In Self Test ensures a higher latent fault coverage for the secondary voltage monitors and the associated alarm and Error Pin (FSP) fault logic routed to Safety Management Unit.
1051137.failure_event: MONBIST fault is detected for main MCU
1051137.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. If ignition cycle change is not performed in 10 minutes VCU will shutdown itself. Vehicle is only able to roll out. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1051137.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues

1051137.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

10619409.description: Right High Beam circuit short to GND
10619409.subsystem: This DTC is detonated if the current is higher than threshold define to this signal, during a determined time.
10619409.failure_event: The output current will be checked to monitor the failure, if the current is higher than specific power threshold(based on HW), it's considered as failure. High Beam Short circuit to GND or Over load for 30ms
10619409.possible_causes: Low visibility for driver if high beam is not activated.
10619409.impact: Low visibility for driver if high beam is not activated.
10619409.limp: Record the DTC Cut off the output Send CAN Message BCM_RiHiBeamFlt=0x1
10619409.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG

1051158.subsystem: This DTC indicates that the voltage supervisor of the Aurix Microcontroller has detected a under voltage condition on the VEXT or VEVRSB rail out of the operating range. This common causes failure which have the potential to affect several safety mechanisms is detected during runtime by the primary or secondary voltage supervisor. The safety mechanisms reports the failure to the SMUstandby which triggers a fails safe reset by the System Basis Chip. If the over voltage condition is present for a longer timespan (not just transient) the ECU enters a deep reset and fail state (internal shutdown) without DTC.
1051158.failure_event: Voltage supervisor of the Aurix Microcontroller has detected a over voltage condition on the VEXT or VEVRSB rail out of the operating range. This common causes failure is detected during runtime.
1051158.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. If ignition cycle change is not performed in 10 minutes VCU will shutdown itself. Vehicle is only able to roll out. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1051158.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU
1051158.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1051158.note: VEXT and VEVRSB in 3V3 Single Supply Mode also the VDDP3 rail are supplied by the Systembasis Chip (external regulator), a violation of the absolute maximum ratings is covered by the diagnostic mechanims of the SBC itself.

1051202.subsystem: This DTC indicates that the Memory Build In Self Test has not been passed successfully and there is a failure in the SRAM. This single point fault is detected during startup in the so called "Memory Build In Self Test" of the Microcontroller executed by L3 SW.
1051202.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. If ignition cycle change is not performed in 10 minutes VCU will shutdown itself. Vehicle is only able to roll out. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1051202.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues
1051202.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1051202.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

1051298.subsystem: This DTC indicates that the voltage supervisor of the Aurix Microcontroller has detected a over voltage condition on the VDD core supply rail out of the operating range. This common causes failure which have the potential to affect several safety mechanisms is detected during runtime by the primary or secondary voltage supervisor. The safety mechanisms reports the failure to the SMUstandby which triggers a fails safe reset by the System Basis Chip. If the over voltage condition is present for a longer timespan (not just transient) the ECU enters a deep reset and fail state (internal shutdown) without DTC.
1051298.failure_event: Voltage supervisor of the Aurix Microcontroller has detected an under voltage condition on the VDD rail out of the operating range. This common causes failure is detected during runtime
1051298.possible_causes: No INV_BUS communication, no HV activation, No Park Lock avaliabe until ignition cycle or automated cold-restart. If ignition cycle change is not performed in 10 minutes VCU will shutdown itself. Vehicle is only able to roll out. Re-activation of HV is first possible after standstill is reached and ignition cycle change.
1051298.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case 2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues
1051298.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1051298.note: The VDD (1V25) voltage is generated by the Aurix EVRC switching regulator istself. The VDDP3 (3V3) is in 5V single supply mode generated by the Aurix EVR33 linear regulator or supplied out of VEXT in 3V3 single supply mode.

Task monitoring is time-shared with the watchdog handler (detection of permanently missing task)

6574244.description: OBD failures that will be set after second occurence
6574244.subsystem: The inertial sensor signal was disturbed by sensor internal failure.
6574244.failure_event: The failure is set if the value of the longitudinal acceleration is less than a specific threshold %{ -15 | m/s^2 | 0 | P_ibax_AxAnschlMax }%.
6574244.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574244.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574245.subsystem: The inertial sensor signal was disturbed by sensor internal failure.
6574245.failure_event: The failure is set if the deviation between measured longitudinal acceleration and longitudinal acceleration calculated by differentiating vehicle reference speed is higher than a specific threshold %{ 36 | % | 2 | fac_a_MinAxOutput*100 }% for a specific time %{ 2 | s | 0 | t_Ax_Output_Filt_Time }%. The failure is only set if vehicle velocity is higher than a specific threshold %{ 6 | m/s | 2 | MAX(v_MINAX,SspWss_v_Rad_Min_GA) }%.
6574245.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574245.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574246.subsystem: The inertial sensor signal was disturbed by sensor internal failure.
6574246.failure_event: The monitoring consists of two parts: 1.) High acceleration monitoring: The failure is set if the longitudinal acceleration is higher than a minimum threshold %{ 0.5 | m/s^2 | 1 | AxsHighAccelerationMonitoringAddThreshold }% added by a threshold depending on velocity %{ 3.5 | m/s^2 | 1 | AxsPlausibilityMonitoringThresholdTable }% for a long time during driving. 2.) Plausibiltiy versus vehicle acceleration: The failure is set if the difference between measured longitudinal acceleration and longitudinal acceleration calculated by differentiating vehicle velocity is higher than a velocity depending threshold %{ 3.5 | m/s^2 | 1 | AxsPlausibilityMonitoringThresholdTable }%.
6574246.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574246.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574247.subsystem: 1.) The inertial sensor signal was disturbed by sensor internal failure. 2.) The inertial sensor signal is faulty because of wrong calibration. 3.) The inertial sensor signal was disturbed by HU misalignment.
6574247.failure_event: The monitoring consists of two parts. 1.) Offset monitoring: The failure is set if the long time filtered longitudinal acceleration value called longitudinal acceleration offset is higher than a specific threshold %{ 2.25 | m/s^2 | 2 | P_axOfsLZAErr }%. Calculation of the longitudinal acceleration offset is done in this way: During normal driving for a long time a symmetrical driving situation is supposed. In this case the mean driving direction is on a plane surface and the long time filtered longitudinal acceleration should be zero. The deviation from the zero position is the longitudinal acceleration offset. The offset is stored in the eeprom over the power cycle. 2.) Missalignment monitoring: The failure is set if the difference between the stored offset value and the new learned offset value is higher than a specific threshold %{ 0.0865 | m/s^2 | 4 | P_MADError/20 }% after a specific learning distance %{ 10000 | m | 0 | (P_WegstrIAx*P_LTCAxIntCount_limit)/20 }%. If the absolute covered distance is lower than a specific threshold %{ 200000 | m | 0 | (P_WegstrIAx*P_LTCAxIntCount_limit) }% the check threshold is increased %{ 1.153 | m/s^2 | 4 | P_MADError/20 }%.
6574247.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574247.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574248.subsystem: 1.) The inertial sensor signal was disturbed by sensor internal failure. 2.) The inertial sensor signal is faulty because of wrong calibration. 3.) The inertial sensor signal was disturbed by HU misalignment.
6574248.failure_event: The monitoring consists of two parts. 1.) Offset monitoring: The failure is set if the long time filtered lateral acceleration value called lateral acceleration offset is higher than a specific threshold %{ 2.25 | m/s^2 | 2 | ayInOfsFineErr }%. Calculation of the lateral acceleration offset is done in this way: During normal driving for a long time a symmetrical driving situation is supposed. In this case the mean driving direction is straight ahead and the long time filtered lateral acceleration should be zero. The deviation from the zero position is the lateral acceleration offset. The offset is stored in the eeprom over the power cycle. 2.) Missalignment monitoring: The failure is set if the new learned offset value has changed by more than a virtually learned offset value after a specific learning distance %{ 10000 | m | 0 | P_MAD_AyDistance }%. The virtually learned offset value corresponds to the partly compensated offset value after the specific learning distance assumed the (non compensated) virtual offset had been a specific value %{ 1.73 | m/s^2 | 4 | P_MADError }% at beginning of the monitoring interval.
6574248.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574248.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574251.description: Out of Range(High) monitoring fault of acceleration signal A1 and A2
6574251.subsystem: The inertial sensor signals A1 or A2 were disturbed by sensor internal failure.
6574251.failure_event: The monitoring consists of two parts. 1.) The failure is set if the acceleration signal A1 is higher than a specific threshold %{ 29 | m/s^2 | 0 | A1ThresholdHigh }%. 2.) The failure is set if the acceleration signal A2 is higher than a specific threshold %{ 29 | m/s^2 | 0 | A2ThresholdHigh }%.
6574251.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574251.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574252.description: Out of Range(Low) monitoring fault of acceleration signal A1 and A2
6574252.subsystem: The inertial sensor signals A1 or A2 were disturbed by sensor internal failure.
6574252.failure_event: The monitoring consists of two parts. 1.) The failure is set if the acceleration signal A1 is less than a specific threshold %{ -29 | m/s^2 | 0 | A1ThresholdHigh }%. 2.) The failure is set if the acceleration signal A2 is less than a specific threshold %{ -29 | m/s^2 | 0 | A2ThresholdHigh }%.
6574252.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574252.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574253.description: Yaw rate signal Out of Range(High)
6574253.subsystem: The Yaw rate signal value is greater than the maximum value plausible for the vehicle
6574253.failure_event: The monitoring checks for a physically plausible yaw rate and consists of following parts: 1.) YrsStandstillRange: The failure is set if the yaw rate is more than 30Â°/s for 0.5s during first standstill after ignition ON. 2.) YrsDriveRange: The failure is set if the yaw rate is more than 94.75Â°/s for 0.8s.
6574253.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574253.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574254.description: Yaw rate signal Out of Range(Low)
6574254.subsystem: The Yaw rate signal value is less than the minimum value plausible for the vehicle
6574254.failure_event: The monitoring checks for a physically plausible yaw rate and consists of following parts: 1.) YrsStandstillRange: The failure is set if the yaw rate is less than -30Â°/s for 0.5s during first standstill after ignition ON. 2.) YrsDriveRange: The failure is set if the yaw rate is less than -94.75Â°/s for 0.8s.
6574254.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574254.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574255.subsystem: The inertial sensor signal was disturbed by sensor internal failure.
6574255.failure_event: The monitoring checks for a physically plausible yaw rate and consists of following parts: 1.) YrsFastOffset1: The failure is set if the yaw rate offset is higher than a specific threshold %{ 0.2622 | rad/s | 4 | P_vGiInOfsFP }%. The yaw rate offset corresponds to the slightly filtered deviation between measured yaw rate and the calculated reference yaw rate. 2.) YrsFastOffset2: The failure is set at the end of the fast compensation and if the offset is higher than a specific threshold %{ 0.1309 | rad/s | 4 | P_vGiOfsMaxFSAbgl }%. The yaw rate offset corresponds to the slightly filtered deviation between measured yaw rate and the calculated reference yaw rate. 3.) Yrs_Gain: The failure is set if the sensitiviy deviation of the yaw rate is higher than a specific threshold %{ 0.25 | | 2 | P_vGiInEmpfErr }%. The yaw rate gain is estimated by comparison of the measured yaw rate and the yaw rate calculated from steering angle and wheel speed sensors during cornering. 4.) Yrs_MonGrad: The failure is set if the signal gradient from one task cycle to another is between specific thresholds %{ 10 | deg/s | 0 | igrd_DiffvGiInUG }% %{ 23 | deg/s | 0 | igrd_DiffvGiInOG }%. The threshold increases while non-stationary driving condition. 5.) Yrs_MonRangeDriving: The failure is set if the absolute value of the yaw rate sensor signal is higher than a specific threshold %{ 94.75 | deg/s | 2 | ibed_vGiAnScndMax-idrs_DrsOffset }%. 6.) Yrs_MonRangeVehStandstill: The failure is set if the absolute value of the yaw rate sensor signal is higher than a specific threshold %{ 30 | deg/s | 0 | ibed_vGiAnFrstMax }%. 7.) Yrs_NormalOffset: The failure is set if the yaw rate offset is higher than a specific threshold %{ 0.1309 | rad/s | 4 | P_vGiOfsMaxFSAbgl }%. The yaw rate offset corresponds to the strong filtered deviation between measured yaw rate and the calculated reference yaw rate. 8.) Yrs_PlausLimVal: The failure is set if the measured and offset compensated yaw rate signal does not follow the reference yaw rate signal. If the measured yaw rate deviates more than a dynamic threshold from the reference yaw rate the quadratic value of the deviation is integrated. The dynamic threshold has a minimum value %{ 0.035 | rad/s | 3 | zvGiRefOversteering }%. The failure is set if the fault integral is higher than a specific threshold %{ 20 | deg/s | 2 | }%. 9.) Yrs_PlausSas: The fault is set if te difference between measured steering angle and model calculated value based on yaw rate signal is higher than a velocity dependent threshold. The bigger the velocity, the lower the threshold. The threshold is between two values %{ 10 | deg | 0 | }% %{ 100 | deg | 0 | }%. The deviation is recognized through three possible recognition paths evaluating stable driving conditions. This is to prevent a fault recognition due to unstable driving. The sensor - yaw rate sensor or steering angle sensor - which is showing the largest deviation to the reference yaw rate is marked as faulty. 10.) Yrs_PlausValidity: The failure is set if the measured and offset compensated yaw rate signal does not follow the reference yaw rate signal. If the deviation is higher than a dynamic threshold a failure is recognized. The dynamic threshold consists of a fix value %{ 0.04363 | rad/s | 5 | zDifvGiErrC1 }% plus situation depending values. 11.) Yrs_StandstillComp: The failure is set if the filtered yaw rate offset is higher than a specific threshold %{ 0.09163 | rad/s | 5 | P_vGiOfsStAbglMax }%. This yaw rate offset is calculated by filtering the measured yaw rate during standstill within a specific time %{ 1.52 | s | 2 | tStAbglSw }% while standstill. At drive off the offset value is checked.

6574540.description: Left ECU motor voltage has the wrong polarity.
6574540.failure_event: If a request for left MGU activation is detected, then the monitoring checks the motor current. Failure is set, if if it falls below a threshold for a defined duration.

6574540.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574541.subsystem: thermic overload of emergency supply switch
6574541.failure_event: The sum of both MGU currents is checked permanently. A too high temperature must be avoided to protect the emergency power stage. With a temperature model the heat up and cool down behaviour is estimated. If the estimated temperature is beyond a critical threshold, then a failure message is generated.
6574541.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574541.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574542.description: The left APB motor stalls during release.
6574542.subsystem: mechanical clamping of MGU or spindle
6574542.failure_event: The MGU current is checked permanently. A too high temperature must be avoided to protect the power stage. With a temperature model the heat up and cool down behaviour is estimated. If the estimated temperature is beyond a critical threshold, then a failure message is generated. During a motor stall the temperature increases rapidely, that means, that a MGU is switched off after a short time.
6574542.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574542.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574758.description: Pressure sensor MC1 Offset is too high

6574758.failure_event: The fault is set if the master cylinder pressure sensor offset monitoring detects a positive or negative offset which is out of the specified signal range ({%default: 15bar(P_pOffsetMax_DS8_1)%}).The zero bar pressure level is at 0.3V and the conversion factor between voltage/pressure is 9.8mV/bar.The offset value is written to EEPROM after 3min and after 15min.

6574758.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6574760.description: Temporary system degradation due to multiple WSS fault suspicion.
6574760.subsystem: Multiple not plausible or invalid WSS signals were detected.
6574760.failure_event: The failure is set if ABS and EBD are released by diagnosis and system management while these controllers are not able to work because required input signals are not available with sufficient quality. This is the case if WSS input signals from less than two wheels are available without assigned fault or fault suspicion for min. 0,5 s consecutively (e.g. the failure is set if at least three WSS show an implausible behavior for 0,5 s). If detection is occurring during an ABS or EBD control on the rear axle, the failure setting is shifted until control on rear axle is finished. The implausibility can be due to different reasons: a) fault assigned to any wheel (ModeFail, SupplyGnd, SupplyHigh, LineHigh, LineGnd, LineUndef, TestFailure, DmaBufNoise, WrongSens, NoEdge, VdaParityBitFail, MonRange, SignalLost, MonVDiff, MonMissingTeeth, MonNoise), or b) implausible WSS signal detected temporarily due to 1.) wheel speed is higher than a specific threshold %{ 82.23 | m/s | 2 | SspWss_SiS_vMax }% for min. 0,5 s. 2.) wheel speed stays at zero after drive off afterwards the vehicle velocity increases a specific threshold %{ 3.3 | m/s | 1 | }%. 3.) wheel speed drops to zero while the vehicle is faster than a specific velocity threshold %{ 3.3 | m/s | 2 | MAX(MAX((SspWss_vDOLO_CheckFailLower*((48/2)/(AnzahlZaehne_VA/Abrollumfang_VA))),SspWss_vDOLO_Check FailLower),SspWss_v_DOHI_Offset)}% and the vehicle does not decelerate below the vehicle speed that was present when the initial wheel speed drop occurred. 4.) wheel acceleration is implausible high, the wheel jerk is implausible high or edges and time information do not match at low speed. 5.) wheel speed differs from other wheel speeds more than a calculated threshold and a varying duration. The threshold is depending on vehicle speed and current system mode, the debounce time is depending on drive situation, current system mode and project specific threshold.
6574760.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6574760.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6575520.subsystem: Wheel speed sensors are swapped at front axle.
6575520.failure_event: "The yaw rate calculated from steering angle and the yaw rates calculated from the wheel speed sensors at both axles are compared. If the yaw rates fit, positive integrals (IvGiVrVZFA and IvGiVrVZRA) are calculated, but if the negative values fit, the integrals will become negative. A fault is determined if the integral value IvGiVrVZFA is negative and a threshold of %{ 1.5708|rad|0| IvorwaertsSw2# }% is exceeded. This fault will cause a release test in the following ignition cycle to assure that the sensors are build in correctly. The conditions for release are IvGiVrVZFA AND IvGiVrVZRA are >= %{ 3.14 | rad | 0 | IvorwaertsSw3# }%.Alternative monitoring without usage of Sas and Yrs, If switches %{ true|-|1| FS_Mbp_WssSignType_WssOnly# }% && %{ false|-|1| FS_Mbp_WssSignType_WssOnly# }%:During stable cornering the wheel speeds are in a defined order which is checked by this monitoring. If the order shows a fault pattern within stable cornering until the failure counter timer_WssSign reaches 125, the fault is set. If an EBD intervention is happening during stable cornering, it is assumed to be a misintervention due to swapped Wss at the rear axle and failure word Wss_SignRA is set. If no EBD misintervention has happened, the failure word Wss_SignFA is set."
6575520.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6575520.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6575521.subsystem: Wheel speed sensors are swapped at rear axle.
6575521.failure_event: "This monitoring detects wrongly mounted wheel speed sensors at rear axle. The yaw rate calculated from steering angle and the yaw rates calculated from the wheel speed sensors at both axles are compared. If the yaw rates fit, positive integrals (IvGiVrVZFA and IvGiVrVZRA) are calculated, but if the negative values fit, the integrals will become negative. A fault is determined if the integral value IvGiVrVZRA is negative and a threshold of %{ 1.5708|rad|0| IvorwaertsSw2# }% is exceeded. This fault will cause a release test in the following ignition cycle to assure that the sensors are build in correctly. The conditions for release are IvGiVrVZFA >= %{ 3.14 | rad | 0 | IvorwaertsSw3# }% AND IvGiVrVZRA >= %{ 3.14 | rad | 0 | IvorwaertsSw3# }% .Alternative monitoring without usage of Sas and Yrs, if switches FS_Mbp_WssSignType_WssOnly == true && IUMPR_MbpSignMonitoring_Active == false:During stable cornering the wheel speeds are in a defined order which is checked by this monitoring. If the order shows a fault pattern within stable cornering until the failure counter timer_WssSign reaches 125, the fault is set. If an EBD intervention is happening during stable cornering, it is assumed to be a misintervention due to swapped Wss at the rear axle and failure word Wss_SignRA is set. If no EBD misintervention has happened, the failure word Wss_SignFA is set."
6575521.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6575521.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6526368.subsystem: Erroneous safety logic (The clockin monitor of the system IC reports wrong clockin failure status to the uC)
6526368.failure_event: The failsafe logic test tests whether the clockin monitor of the system IC detects a too high, a too low or a correct clockin frequency. This failure is logged if the feedback to the uC does not show the expected clockin failure state.
6526368.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6526368.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6526369.subsystem: Erroneous safety logic (the BIST state machine of the system chip could be activated by a wrong BIST command value)
6526369.failure_event: The failsafe logic test tests whether the BIST state machine does detect and suppress a wrong BIST command value. This failure is logged if the watchdog of the system chip is triggered by the wrong BIST command value.
6526369.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6526369.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6526370.subsystem: Erroneous safety logic (e.g. BIST of system IC either does not detect a correct BIST command or missing BIST commands)
6526370.failure_event: The failsafe logic test tests whether the watchdog of the system chip can be started with the first watchdog trigger signal from the BIST state machine. Additionally it is tested that the watchdog switches off hydraulically respective electrically if the BIST state machine is not triggered during the corresponding timeout times.
6526370.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6526370.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6526371.subsystem: Erroneous safety logic (BIST of system IC does not switch off electrically after too much received wrong BIST commands)
6526371.failure_event: The failsafe logic test tests that after 3 wrong BIST commands the BIST failure counter counted up to its max. value and that the BIST state machine switches off electrically consequently.
6526371.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6526371.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6526372.subsystem: Error pin event counter within system chip does not increment on error pin event.
6526372.failure_event: The failsafe logic test pulls the error pin to test the error pin event counter within the system chip.
6526372.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6526372.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6526374.subsystem: System chip internal decouple bits are not reset within the expected time.

6526374.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6575780.subsystem: Huge tolerance in assembly position of the sensor or wrong wiring or internal offset or sensitivity fault of the sensor.
6575780.failure_event: The monitoring checks for a physically plausible lateral acceleration signal and consists of following parts:1) Monitoring during validity:By building a reference lateral acceleration from all available sensor signals - steering angle - yaw rate - lateral acceleration - wheel speed sensors - it is possible to test the lateral acceleration(Ay)-signal on plausibility. If during stable vehicle behaviour a lateral acceleration failure larger than approximately a sum of %{ 2.0 | m/sÂ2 | 1 | zDifAyErrC1}% and needed increase in tolerance based on the situation occurs, the failure condition is fulfilled and a fault is recognized during straight ahead driving, when the failure counter tayFV1 reaches %{1600 | ms | 0 | tayFV1Sw * 40 }% during model validity.During cornering only the failure counterincreases and when the threshold is reached the vehicle dynamics controller will disregard the sensor information so that a false intervention is prevented.2) Monitoring during model observability:By building a reference lateral acceleration from all available sensor signals - steering angle - yaw rate - lateral acceleration - wheel speed sensors - it is possible to test the lateral acceleration signal on plausibility. If during stable vehicle behavior a lateral acceleration failure larger than approximately %{ 2.0 | m/sÂ2 | 1 | zDifAyErrC1}% and needed increase in tolerance based on the situation occurs, after a time of %{120 | ms | 0 | tayFV2Sw1*40}% the vehicle dynamic controller will disregard the sensor information so that a false vehicle dynamic intervention is prevented. During observability the recognition time depends on the amount of failure.The deviation of the measured signal from the model values is integrated when exceeding the allowed threshold. The shut down threshold for the fault integral IBeta is %{ 4.363 | 1/s | 2 | IBetaMax*(3.1416/180)/0.04 }% .During cornering only the fault integral increases, fault is only set during straight ahead driving.
6575780.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6575780.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6575785.subsystem: Wrong wiring or wrong assembly position of the sensor.
6575785.failure_event: This monitoring detects a wrong sign of yaw rate signal. The measured yaw rate and the model yaw rate calculated from the steering angle are compared. During forward driving these signals must have the same sign. If the measured yaw rate fits to the model yaw rate from steering angle, positive integrals (Ivorwaerts,IvGiVrFA,IvGiVrRA ) is calculated but if the negative value fits the integral will become negative. A fault is determined if the integral value of the measured yaw rate(Ivorwaerts) is negative and smaller than %{ -0.5236 | rad | 2 | IvorwaertsSw1# }%, forward driving is recognized either by reaching a speed threshold close to maximum possible reverse speed %{ 19.5 | m/s | 1 | P_vFzRefVorwSw # }% or by intelligent wheel speed sensors direction information and the integrals of the model yaw rates obtained from wheel speeds at front and rear axle(IvGiVrFA, IvGiVrRA) are greater than %{0.5236 | rad/s | 2 | IvorwaertsSw1#}%. This fault requires a release check in the following ignition cycle to prevent system release when the fault is still present. The conditions for release are positive integral(Ivorwaerts) > +180 deg AND after that the vehicle drives faster than P_Vfric (default value: 20 m/s).
6575785.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6575785.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6575786.subsystem: The iBooster is Off or in Failure mode

6575786.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG

6575787.subsystem: Wrong assembly position of the steering angle sensor or swapped wheel speed sensors.
6575787.failure_event: This monitoring detects a wrong sign of steering angle signal. The yaw rate calculated from steering angle, the measured yaw rate and the yaw rates calculated from the wheel speed sensors are compared. If the measured yaw rate and the wheel speeds based yaw rate models fit to the model yaw rate from steering angle, positive integrals (Ivorwaerts, IvGiVrFA and IvGiVrRA) are calculated, but if the negative values fits, the integrals will become negative. A fault is determined if the integral values are negative and smaller than %{ 0.52 | rad | 2 | IvorwaertsSw1# }% and forward driving is recognized either by reaching a speed threshold close to maximum possible reverse speed %{ 19.5 | m/s | 1 | P_vFzRefVorwSw # }% or by intelligent wheel speed sensors direction information. This fault can also be determined only by wheel speed based integrals IvGiVrRA and IvGiVrFA < %{ 1.57 | rad | 2 | IvorwaertsSw2# }% indipendent of the driving direction. It requires a release check in the following ignition cycle to prevent system release when the fault is still present.
6575787.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6575787.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

1152755.subsystem: The failure event "EVENT_L3_MU_nSSP3_CHECK" is set: #### PATH1 - SSP2 Pull Up Resistor Check #### 1a.) the readback value of the SSP2 MCU output is not "high" as expected while the MCU open collector output port is not driven to "low" (SSP2_Fb == 0b) #### PATH2 - nSSP3 Initial State Check #### 1b1.) or the readback value of the safe state path 3 via MCU port input is not "asserted" as expected although the safe state output is not yet released (neither MCU SSP2 nor SBC FS0b) || (nSSP3_Fb == released) 1b2.) or the readback value of the safe state path 3 via SPI from the SBC is not "low" as expected although the safe state output is not yet released (neither MCU SSP2 nor SBC FS0b) || (nSSP3_FS0b_SNS == 1) #### PATH3 - nSSP3 Low State Check - Asserted by FS65xx #### 1c.) or the readback value of the safe state path 3 via MCU port input is not "asserted" as expected although the safe state output is not yet released (still asserted by SBC FS0b while MCU SSP2 is already released) || (nSSP3_Fb == released) #### PATH4 - nSSP3 High State Check - Released by FS65xx #### 1d1.) or the readback value of the safe state path 3 via MCU port input is not "released" as expected although the safe state output is released (by MCU SSP2 and SBC FS0b) || (nSSP3_Fb == asserted) 1d2.) or the readback value of the safe state path 3 via SPI from the SBC is not "high" as expected although the safe state output is released (by MCU SSP2 and SBC FS0b) || (nSSP3_FS0b_SNS == 0) #### PATH5 - nSSP3 Low State Check - Asserted by MCU #### 1e.) or the readback value of the safe state path 3 via MCU port input is not "asserted" as expected although the safe state output is not yet released (still asserted by MCU SSP2 while SBC FS0b is already released) || (nSSP3_Fb == released) #### PATH6 - SAFE_STATE_REQUESTED not executed #### 1.) if the RSTb bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Reset"

10622741.description: Left front LED turn light broken, or feedback line short to battery, or feedback line open load
10622741.subsystem: The DTC is triggered by feedback lines of the turn indicator lamp embedded logic. It can represent line broken, STB, STG, Open Circuit.
10622741.failure_event: 100ms after left turn indicator is turned ON and while it remains ON, the Left Front feedback line is read as HIGH for 200ms.
10622741.possible_causes: Driver will not be able to signal other drivers intention to change lanes.
10622741.impact: Driver will not be able to signal other drivers intention to change lanes.
10622741.limp: Record the DTC Use double frequency blinking for left turn indicators and left side markers for requests other than Hazard lights Transmit BCM_LeTrunLampOutpCmd with double frequency toggling. Send CAN Message BCM_LeTurnLampFlt=0x1
10622741.steps: Check circuit of turn indicator lamps
10622741.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
10622741.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).
10622741.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Voltage supply of IBC is in the range of 11-16V Turn Left Light active for 100ms Continuous 20ms Trigger Reset Age 100ms after left turn indicator is turned ON and while it remains ON, the Left Front feedback line is read as HIGH for 200ms. Next activation, 100ms after left turned indicator is turn ON and while it remains ON, the Left Front feedback line is read as LOW for 200ms. After 100 IGN ON to OFF cycles,clear history DTC automatically

1153284.subsystem: The failure event "EVENT_L3_MU_BIST" is set: 1a.) if the LBIST_OK bit of the BIST register reports a "Logic Bild in Self Test failure" [(BIST: LBIST_OK == 0) 1b.) or the ABIST1_OK bit of the BIST register reports a "Analog Build in Self Test 1 failure" and the Fail Safe Output has no short circuit to high" || (BIST: ABIST1_OK == 0) 1c.) or the ABIST2_VAUX_OK bit of the BIST register reports a "Analog Build in Self Test 2 - VAUX failure or not executed" if VAUX will be used safety critical || ((BIST: ABIST2_VAUX_OK == 0) && ((VAUX_FS_OV_1:0 != 00) || (VAUX_FS_UV_1:0 != 00)) 1d.) or if a device with FS1B output is present and the ABIST2_FS1B_OK bit of the BIST register reports a "Analog Build in Self Test 2 - FS1b failure or not executed" (BIST: ABIST2_FS1B == 0) && (DEVICE_ID_FS.FS1 == 1)]
1153284.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues
