1153538.subsystem: The failure event "EVENT_L3_MCU_SMU_BUS" is set: 1.) if the RSTb bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Reset" (WD_ANSWER: RSTB == 1) 2.) and the FSxB bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Fail-safe event" && (WD_ANSWER: FSxB == 1) 3a.) and either the IO23_FAIL bit of the DIAG_FS_IOs register reports an "IO23 monitoring error" && ((DIAG_SF_IOs: IO_23_FAIL == 1) 3b.) or the reset proof RAM marker indicates that the wrong WD data refresh was provoked due to a not executed SMU reaction (e.g. SMU_FSP stuck at high) || (RPRAM_IntendedWD_Error)) #### Path 1 - SRI / End to End EDC CPU 0/1 #### 4a.) a "CPU0 Instruction Fetch SRI Interface EDC Error" is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register (RP_RAM_SMU_AG0.SF22) 4b.) a "CPU1 Instruction Fetch SRI Interface EDC Error" is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RP_RAM_SMU_AG1.SF22) 4c.) a "CPU0 Data SRI Interface (Load/Store) EDC Error" is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RP_RAM_SMU_AG0.SF23) 4d.) a "CPU1 Data SRI Interface (Load/Store) EDC Error" is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RP_RAM_SMU_AG1.SF23) 4e.) a "XBAR0 Bus Error Event / EndInit / SafteyEndInit Register Protection Event" is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RP_RAM_SMU_AG7.SF17) #### Path 2 - SPB Error Detection #### 4f.) a "SPB Bus Error Event / / EndInit / SafteyEndInit Register Protection Event" is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RP_RAM_SMU_AG7.SF20) #### Path 3 - DMA SRI ECC #### 4g.) a "DMA SRI ECC Error" is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RP_RAM_SMU_AG8.SF23)

1153540.subsystem: The failure event "EVENT_L3_MCU_LBIST" is set if: ##### Path 1 - direct after execution during Cold Porst ##### 1a.) the MISR signature read from the LBIST3 Control Registers is not equal the expected value (LBISTCTRL3.SIGNATURE != 0x4072038C) 1b.) or the "LBIST Execution Indicator" doesn't indicate "that the LBIST procedure has been successfully finished since last power on reset" within a specified time span || (SCU_LBISTCTRL0.LBISTDONE != 1) 1c.) or the LBPORST bit in the Reset Status Register indicates that the LBIST execution was terminated earlier due to a PORST assertion || (SCU_RSTSTAT.LBPORST == 1) 1d.) or the LBTERM bit in the Reset Status Register indicates that the LBIST execution was not terminated properly || (SCU_RSTSTAT.LBTERM == 0) ##### Path 2 - after SBC Init Timeout ##### if the MCU is starting up after a "SBC reset caused by a Init Timeout" and the "LBIST after the Cold porst before was not successfull" 2a.) (RPRAM_LBIST_Result != 0xA5A5A5A5) && (RPRAM_SBC_InitTimeout == 1)
1153540.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent)
1153540.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG

10623505.description: Right front LED turn light feedback line short to ground
10623505.subsystem: The DTC is triggered by the status of the logical input of the turn indicator's feedback line.
10623505.failure_event: The BCM shall consider that the Right Front Turn Indicator Feedback is in short-to-ground if 100ms after Turn Indicator Right is turned OFF and while it remains in OFF, the feedback line input is read as LOW for 200ms.
10623505.possible_causes: The status of the Turn Indicator Lamp may not be accuretly detected by BCM.
10623505.impact: The status of the Turn Indicator Lamp may not be accuretly detected by BCM.
10623505.limp: Record the DTC Send CAN Message BCM_RiTurnLampFlt=0x1
10623505.steps: Check circuit of turn indicator lamps

10623505.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

14211620.description: Seat Height Adjustment Motor - Circuit current below threshold/open load
14211620.cause: Current is below the minimum threshold for more than 500ms.
14211620.impact: Seat can't be moved on this axis.
14211620.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the regular CAN messages.
14211620.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (seat must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is below the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14211620.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age The current drawn by the motor is below the minimum threshold of tbd V. This indicates a broken cable motor. Current drawn by the motor is above the minimum of V. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

6576801.subsystem: 1.) The SAS signal is faulty because of defective steering angle sensor.2.) The SAS signal is faulty because of CAN/Flexray failure.
6576801.failure_event: The failure is set if SAS sensor transmits more than 3 or less than 1 message(s) via bus communication refered to a cycle time of 20 ms.

6576802.subsystem: 1.) The SAS signal is faulty because of defective steering angle sensor.2.) The SAS signal is faulty because of wrong sensor mounting.3.) The SAS is not calibrated yet (Class 2&3).4.) The SAS signal is faulty because of wheel speed sensors interchange (Class 1: learning condition not fulfilled).5.) The SAS signal is invalid because of failure in YRS signal (Class 1: learning condition not fulfilled).6.) The SAS signal is faulty because of geometrical failure at steering mechanic (Class 1: learning condition not fulfilled).
6576802.failure_event: The failure is set1.) if the steering angle sensor Class 3 is not initialized or calibrated or if the center of SAS Class 1 was not found AND2.) a.) if in init phase the vehicle speed is higher than a minimum threshold %{ 0 | m/s | 2 | vFzRefXFAMin # }% and the center was not found until a specific time %{ 0 | s | 2 | XFAFailureTimeDelay # }% OR b.) if in init phase the vehicle speed is higher than a maximum threshold %{ 0 | m/s | 2 | vFzRefXFAMax # }% ORc.) if SAS signal was available before in the ignition cycle and the vehicle speed is higher than threshold %{ 0 | m/s | 2 | vFzRefXFAInitLoss # }%. The failure will be reset as soon as the SAS Class 3 is initialized and calibrated or for SAS Class 1 the center is found.
6576802.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6576802.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6576803.description: OBD failures that will be set after second occurence
6576803.subsystem: 1.) The SAS signal is faulty because of defective steering angle sensor.2.) The SAS signal is faulty because of wrong sensor mounting.
6576803.failure_event: The monitoring checks for a physically plausible steering angle signal range in case of right steering. The failure is set after the received raw sensor signal is higher than a specific threshold %{ 810 | deg | 0 | LwAnschlMax+LwAnschlZuschlag # }%. This threshold is the sum of the maximum, physical steering wheel turn from zero-position to steering stop in the vehicle and an additional robustness tolerance value.
6576803.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6576803.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6576804.description: OBD failures that will be set after second occurence
6576804.subsystem: 1.) The SAS signal is faulty because of defective steering angle sensor.2.) The SAS signal is faulty because of wrong sensor mounting.
6576804.failure_event: The monitoring checks for a physically plausible steering angle signal range in case of left steering. The failure is set after the received raw sensor signal is lower than minus a specific threshold %{ 810 | deg | 0 | LwAnschlMax+LwAnschlZuschlag # }%. This threshold is the sum of the maximum, physical steering wheel turn from zero-position to steering stop in the vehicle and an additional robustness tolerance value.
6576804.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6576804.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6576805.subsystem: 1.) The SAS signal is faulty because of defective steering angle sensor.2.) The SAS signal is faulty because of wrong sensor mounting.3.) The SAS signal is faulty because of wheel speed sensors interchange (Class 1: learning condition not fulfilled).4.) The SAS signal is invalid because of failure in YRS signal (Class 1: learning condition not fulfilled).
6576805.failure_event: The monitoring checks for a physically plausible steering angle signal and consists of following parts:1.) Sas gradient monitoring: The failure is set in three different situations:a.) if the signal gradient (steering angle velocity) from one task cycle to another is higher than a specific threshold %{ 60 | deg | 0 | LwInGradMax # }% for defined number of SAS Messages %{ 2 | | 0 | msmc_LwsDifMc }%. b.) if the signal gradient (steering angle velocity) from one task cycle to another is higher than specific threshold %{ 90 | deg | 0 | LwInGradMax2 # }% for more than a defined number of SAS messages %{ 3 | | 0 | msmc_LwsDifMcOg }%.c.) if the steering angle signal acceleration is higher than a specific threshold %{ 30 | deg | 0 | LwInGradDiffMax # }%.2.) Sas plausibility versus yaw rate: The failure is set if the difference between measured steering angle and model calculated value based on yaw rate signal is higher than a velocity dependent threshold. The bigger the velocity, the lower the threshold. The threshold is between two values %{ 10 | deg | 0 | }% %{ 100 | deg | 0 | }%. The deviation is recognized through three possible recognition paths evaluating stable driving conditions. This is to prevent a fault recognition due to unstable driving. The sensor - yaw rate sensor or steering angle sensor - which is showing the largest deviation to the reference yaw rate is marked as faulty.
6576805.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6576805.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6576806.subsystem: 1.) The SAS signal is faulty because of defective steering angle sensor.2.) The SAS signal is faulty because of wrong sensor mounting.3.) The SAS is faulty calibrated (end of line).4.) The SAS signal is faulty because of wheel speed sensors interchange (Class 1: learning condition not fulfilled).5.) The SAS signal is invalid because of failure in YRS signal.6.) The SAS signal is faulty because of geometrical failure at steering mechanic (Class 1: learning condition not fulfilled).
6576806.failure_event: The failure is set if the long time filtered steering angle value called steering angle offset is higher than a specific threshold %{ 15 | deg | 0 | P_LtcSasSensorOffset_Max }%. Calculation of the steering angle offset is done in this way: During normal driving for a long time a symmetrical driving situation is supposed. In this case the mean driving direction is straight ahead and the long time filtered steering angle should be zero. The deviation from the zero position is the steering angle offset. The offset is stored in the EEPROM over the power cycle.
6576806.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6576806.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6576807.subsystem: 1.) The SAS signal is faulty because of defective steering angle sensor.2.) The SAS signal is faulty because of CAN/Flexray failure.
6576807.failure_event: The failure is set if the change of steering angle signal is less than a specific value %{ 5 | deg | 0 | P_DifLwMinMax # }% but both right and left cornering (since last vehicle stand-still situation) was detected.
6576807.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6576807.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

1153776.subsystem: The failure event "EVENT_L3_MCU_UNEXPECTED_RESET" is set if: #### Path 1 - Reset due to IO2 - without information in the SMU Alarm Registers ##### 1a.) if the RSTb bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Reset" (WD_ANSWER: RSTB == 1) 2a.) and the RSTb_EXT bit of the DIAG_SF_IOs register doesn't report a " external Reset" (DIAG_SF_IOs: RSTB_EXT == 0) 3a.) and the FSxB bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Fail-safe event" && (WD_ANSWER: FSxB == 1) 4a.) and the IO23_FAIL bit of the DIAG_FS_IOs register reports an "IO23 monitoring error" && (DIAG_SF_IOs: IO_23_FAIL == 1) 5a.) and no Alarm is present in SMU_Stdby && ((SMU_AG20.SFx == 0) && (SMU_AG21.SFx == 0)) 6a.) and no Alarm is present in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core && ((RPRAM_SMU_AG0.SFx == 0) && (RPRAM_SMU_AG1.SFx == 0) && (RPRAM_SMU_AG6.SFx == 0) && (RPRAM_SMU_AG7.SFx == 0) && (RPRAM_SMU_AG8.SFx == 0) && (RPRAM_SMU_AG9.SFx == 0) && (RPRAM_SMU_AG10.SFx == 0)) #### Path 2 - Reset due to IO2 or WD Refresh a cascading reset of an unexpected system or application reset ##### 1b.) if the RSTb bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Reset" (WD_ANSWER: RSTB == 1) 2b.) and the FSxB bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Fail-safe event" && (WD_ANSWER: FSxB == 1) 3b.) and the IO23_FAIL bit reports an "IO23 monitoring error" or the WD_BAD_TIMING bit reports a "WD refresh in the wrong WD window" && ((DIAG_SF_IOs: IO_23_FAIL == 1) || (WD_ANSWER: WD_BAD_TIMING == 1)) 4b.) and the reset proof RAM flag which indicates a system or application reset is set && (RPRAM_UnexpectedResetFlag == true) *** Note***: the flag RPRAM_UnexpectedResetFlag is set if "Start-up Memory Registers" indicates that the MCU ist starting up from a "System Reset" or "Application Reset" (SCU_STMEM3.APPR_CS == 1) || (SCU_STMEM3.SYSR_CS == 1)

1153777.subsystem: The failure event "EVENT_L3_MCU_ALIVE_ALARM" is set if: 1.) the "SMU_core Alarm Status flag" in the "SMU_stdby Alarm Status Register" doesn't indicate an "smu_core_alive_alarm" (SMU_AG21_STDBY: SF16 == 0)
1153777.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>) 2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECU special measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues
1153777.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1153777.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

1153784.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) replace the ECU (Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description) Case2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent) 2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>) 2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>)

1153785.subsystem: The failure event "EVENT_L3_MCU_SMU_SAFETY_FLIP_FLOP" is set: 1.) if the RSTb bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Reset" (WD_ANSWER: RSTB == 1) 2.) and the FSxB bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Fail-safe event" && (WD_ANSWER: FSxB == 1) 3a.) and either the IO23_FAIL bit of the DIAG_FS_IOs register reports an "IO23 monitoring error" && ((DIAG_SF_IOs: IO_23_FAIL == 1) 3b.) or the reset proof RAM marker indicates that the wrong WD data refresh was provoked due to a not executed SMU reaction (e.g. SMU_FSP stuck at high) || (RPRAM_IntendedWD_Error)) ### Path 1 - PMS Safety flip-flops #### 4a.) a "PMS Safety flip-flop uncorrectable error alarm is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register (RPRAM_SMU_AG6.SF5 == 1) 4b.) or a "Miscellaneous safety flip flop uncorrectable error alarm is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RPRAM_SMU_AG10.SF21 == 1) #### Path 2 - Clock Safety flip-flops #### 4c.) or a "SYS_PLL / PER_PLL safety flip flop alarm is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RPRAM_SMU_AG6.SF8 == 1) 4d.) or a "CCU safety flip flop uncorrectable error alarm is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RPRAM_SMU_AG6.SF24 == 1) 4e.) or a "CCU safety flip flop uncorrectable error alarm is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RPRAM_SMU_AG6.SF25 == 1) 4f.) or a "CCU safety flip flop uncorrectable error alarm is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm Status Register || (RPRAM_SMU_AG10.SF20 == 1) #### Path 3 - Further Safety flip-flops #### 4g.) or a "MTU safety flip flop uncorrectable error alarm is indicated in the reset proof RAM copy of the SMU_Core Alarm

1153790.subsystem: The failure event "EVENT_L3_MCU_DTS_TEMP_CROSS" is set: 1.) if the difference between the "Core Tempetrature" and the "PMS Temperature" is higher than a threshold ABS (MCU_PMS_Temperature - MCU_CORE_Temperature) > P_MCU_DTSCrossCheckLim 2.) and the difference is present for a timespan longer than a filtertime ** && (t_DTSDebounceCrossCheck >= P_MCU_DTSDebounceCrossCheck)

10623765.impact: Driver will not be able to signal other drivers intention to change lanes.
10623765.limp: Record the DTC Use double frequency blinking for right turn indicators and right side markers for requests other than Hazard lights Transmit BCM_RiTrunLampOutpCmd with double frequency toggling. Send CAN Message BCM_RiTurnLampFlt=0x1

10623765.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
10623765.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

14211877.description: Seat Length Adjustment Motor - Circuit current below threshold/open load
14211877.cause: Current is below the minimum threshold for more than 500ms.
14211877.impact: Seat can't be moved on this axis.
14211877.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the regular CAN messages.
14211877.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (seat must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is below the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14211877.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age The current drawn by the motor is below the minimum threshold of tbd V. This indicates a broken cable motor. Current drawn by the motor is above the minimum of V. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

1153857.subsystem: The failure event "EVENT_L3_FLASH_CONSISTENCY" is set: 1.) if the RSTb bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Reset" (WD_ANSWER: RSTB == 1) 2.) and the FSxB bit of the WD ANSWER register reports a "Fail-safe event" && (WD_ANSWER: FSxB == 1) 3a.) and either the IO23_FAIL bit of the DIAG_FS_IOs register reports an "IO23 monitoring error" && ((DIAG_SF_IOs: IO_23_FAIL == 1) 3b.) or the reset proof RAM marker indicates that the wrong WD data refresh was provoked due to a not executed SMU reaction (e.g. SMU_FSP stuck at high) || (RPRAM_IntendedWD_Error)) 4a.) and the Monitoring Function has triggered the "SW Alarm 7" which is reported in the SMU_core Alarm Status Register && (SMU_AG10.SF7 ==1) 4b.) The calculated CRC value of a block is unequal to the expected value. The check is done for application and dataset. * number of checked elements is not equal to table entries * calculated CRC32 for sector is not equal to stored one
1153857.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent 1.a) update or reflash the ECU

6527908.subsystem: Splitter or blade causing short-circuit between UEXS and VPRE.
6527908.failure_event: Test evaluates UEXS (external 5V sensor supply) undervoltage bit after ASIC power-on in order to detect a short-circuit between VPRE (internal voltage pre-regulator) and UEXS (neighbor pins). If the bit is missing and an ADC is available on the UEXS path a SW-equivalent of the ASIC test will be executed. UEXS is switched off, after 20ms voltage on UEXS path has to drop below threshold.
6527908.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6527908.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6527909.description: Charge-pump tests/monitoring could not be executed because of undervoltage.

6527909.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6527909.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6527910.subsystem: High load at UEXS e.g. due to external short-circuit.
6527910.failure_event: Monitor checks UEXS (external 5V sensor supply) over-temperature bit and switches off UEXS to avoid a toggling system in case of an error. After a cool-down phase UEXS is switched on again to re-check if the failure is still present - if it is not the failure will be healed. Once the switch on limit is reached it's not possible re-check in the current power cycle.
6527910.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6527910.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

1154043.subsystem: The failure will be set: 1. Layout Version mismatch detected 2. No complete sector, sector switch count indicates initialisation phase => interrupted NVM initialisation - reinitialize * 3. Find next Block Header is not possible (Read failed) 4. Block head read out invalid class Id 5. Block data read is not possible (Read failed) 6. Block data CRC failure 7. Header pattern is not as expected
1154043.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent1.a) replace the ECU(Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description)Case2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent)2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>)2.b.) Perform test of the system functionality (<customer specific>)2.c.) read again error memory entry (<customer specific>), if error memory entry is still present - replace the ECUspecial measure: SW update (potential errata) in case of SW dependant known issues
1154043.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1154043.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

1154044.subsystem: The failure will be set: If consecutive times a voltage loss on KL30/KL30 happends (line break), in that case the shutdown procedure is not successful finished. The count of consecutive voltage loss is a parameter.
1154044.steps: Case 1.) if error occurrence counter >=3 or if the failure is permanent1.a) check wiring harness and connector(s)1.b) check other ECU for low voltage events(Control units commissioning as specified in technical product description)Case2.) otherwise (error occurrence counter <= 2 and failure is not permanent)2.a.) Delete error memory entry (<customer specific>)2.b.) 14212134.description: Seat Backrest Adjustment Motor - Circuit current below threshold/open load
14212134.cause: Current is below the minimum threshold for more than 500ms.
14212134.impact: Seat can't be moved on this axis.
14212134.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the regular CAN messages.
14212134.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (seat must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is below the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14212134.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age The current drawn by the motor is below the minimum threshold of tbd V. This indicates a broken cable motor. Current drawn by the motor is above the minimum of V. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14212391.description: Seat Height Adjustment Motor - Circuit current above threshold/short to GND
14212391.cause: Current is above the maximum threshold for more than 500ms.
14212391.impact: Seat can't be moved on this axis. Risk of thermal event and destruction of the electrical circuitry.
14212391.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the
14212391.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. Check for scrubbed free cables. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (seat must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is above the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14212391.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age The current drawn by the motor is above the maximum threshold of tbd V. This indicates a broken cable motor. Current drawn by the motor is below the maximum threshold of V. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

6397449.description: USS - Front Inner Right (FIR) Sensor Component Failure
6397449.subsystem: Failure in the power supply chain/connection error
6397449.failure_event: The Hydra3_component shall log the DTC if it receives any one of the following Flags from FIR sensor and flag present for 400 ms: 1. FIR_hardware_Fault OR 2. FIR_OverTemp_fault OR 3. FIR_comm_Fault OR 4. FIR_CommUnknown OR 5. FIR_SNR_Firmware_Mismatch OR 6. FIR_Blockage
6397449.limp: Following features will not be available: Full Automatic Park Assist System (APA), Personal Park Assist, Remote Parking Assist Following Feature will be degraded : Automatic Cruise Control (ACC) Including Stop and Go
6397449.steps: 1. Check connectors 2. Check FIR sensor 3. Disconnect and re-connect FIR sensor 4. Ensure stable power supply to Front USS sensor 5. If problem still exists, replace FIL sensor

14212648.description: Seat Length Adjustment Motor - Circuit current above threshold/short to GND
14212648.cause: Current is above the maximum threshold for more than 500ms.
14212648.impact: Seat can't be moved on this axis. Risk of thermal event and destruction of the electrical circuitry.
14212648.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the
14212648.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. Check for scrubbed free cables. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (seat must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is above the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14212648.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age The current drawn by the motor is above the maximum threshold of tbd V. This indicates a broken cable motor. Current drawn by the motor is below the maximum threshold of V. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14212905.description: Seat Backrest Adjustment Motor - Circuit current above threshold/short to GND
14212905.cause: Current is above the maximum threshold for more than 500ms.
14212905.impact: Seat can't be moved on this axis. Risk of thermal event and destruction of the electrical circuitry.
14212905.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the
14212905.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. Check for scrubbed free cables. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (seat must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is above the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14212905.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age The current drawn by the motor is above the maximum threshold of tbd V. This indicates a broken cable motor. Current drawn by the motor is below the maximum threshold of V. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

ESP_WhlMovgDir_RL is not 0x0 or 0x1 or 0x2 or 0x3. - Ref to DBC file for Signal Value Description. - Continuous detected for 100ms.

6397961.failure_event: The Hydra3_component shall log the DTC if any one of the following conditions is met - 1. RADAR_INFO (0x300)||OBJ_DATA_00 (0x310)||OBJ_DATA_01 (0x311)||OBJ_DATA_02 (0x312)||OBJ_DATA_03 (0x313)||OBJ_DATA_04 (0x314)||OBJ_DATA_05 (0x315)||OBJ_DATA_06 (0x316)||OBJ_DATA_07 (0x317)||OBJ_DATA_08 (0x318)||OBJ_DATA_09 (0x319)||OBJ_DATA_10 (0x31A)||OBJ_DATA_11 (0x31B)||OBJ_DATA_12 (0x31C)||OBJ_DATA_13 (0x31D)||OBJ_DATA_14 (0x31E)||OBJ_DATA_15 (0x31F)||OBJ_DATA_16 (0x320)||OBJ_DATA_17 (0x321)||OBJ_DATA_18 (0x322)||OBJ_DATA_19 (0x323)||OBJ_DATA_20 (0x324)||OBJ_DATA_21 (0x325)||OBJ_DATA_22 (0x326)||OBJ_DATA_23 (0x327)||OBJ_DATA_24 (0x328)||OBJ_DATA_25 (0x329)||OBJ_DATA_26 (0x32A)||OBJ_DATA_27 (0x32B)||OBJ_DATA_28 (0x32C)||OBJ_DATA_29 (0x32D)||OBJ_DATA_30 (0x32E)||OBJ_DATA_31 (0x32F)||ROAD_BORDER (0x500) message missing for 650 msec OR 2. RADAR_CFG (0x600) message missing for 10 sec OR 3. Hydra3 receives SensorStatus - 0x7 (SENSOR_ST_EXTERNAL_ERROR) from RADAR_INFO (0x300) message and it is valid for 650 msec OR 4. After setting any of the below NRCs, the diagnostic tool will re-attempt (3 times) to flash MRR through Hydra3 and fails a. Subfunction not supported ($12) b. Incorrect message/data length or invalid format ($13) c. Flashing conditions not correct (vehicle speed/ECU voltage) ($22) d. Incorrect sequence ($24) e. Request out of range ($31) f. Security Access denied ($33) g. General Programming Failure ($72) OR 5. Re-flashing halts or aborts programming midway- MRR accepts the reprogramming request, but during the flash, the software suddenly halts and installation is incomplete. OR 6. Flashing is complete, but MRR malfunctions due to error in software OR 7. For the message RADAR_INFO (0x300)||OBJ_DATA_00 (0x310)||OBJ_DATA_01 (0x311)||OBJ_DATA_02 (0x312)||OBJ_DATA_03 (0x313)||OBJ_DATA_04 (0x314)||OBJ_DATA_05 (0x315)||OBJ_DATA_06 (0x316)||OBJ_DATA_07 (0x317)||OBJ_DATA_08 (0x318)||OBJ_DATA_09 (0x319)||OBJ_DATA_10 (0x31A)||OBJ_DATA_11 (0x31B)||OBJ_DATA_12 (0x31C)||OBJ_DATA_13 (0x31D)||OBJ_DATA_14 (0x31E)||OBJ_DATA_15 (0x31F)||OBJ_DATA_16 (0x320)||OBJ_DATA_17 (0x321)||OBJ_DATA_18 (0x322)||OBJ_DATA_19 (0x323)||OBJ_DATA_20 (0x324)||OBJ_DATA_21 (0x325)||OBJ_DATA_22 (0x326)||OBJ_DATA_23 (0x327)||OBJ_DATA_24 (0x328)||OBJ_DATA_25 (0x329)||OBJ_DATA_26 (0x32A)||OBJ_DATA_27 (0x32B)||OBJ_DATA_28 (0x32C)||OBJ_DATA_29 (0x32D)||OBJ_DATA_30 (0x32E)||OBJ_DATA_31 (0x32F)||ROAD_BORDER (0x500)||RADAR_CFG (0x600), if - CRC verification error occurs in the last 3 consecutive messages OR - Maximum difference of Counter value (MaxDeltaCounterInit) > 1 for 3 times continuously OR - Counter value is repeated in the last 3 consecutive messages OR - MAC verification error occurs in the last 3 consecutive messages OR 8. Front Center MRR role and scan config is incorrect during start up i.e. Hydra3 receives RadarRoleConfig != 0x1 (RadarRoleConfig_ROLE_FRONT) from message RADAR_CFG (0x600) during Start up via HSCAN2.

6398217.subsystem: 1. Harness defect 2. Camera defect 3. Camera is not powered as specified by ECU
6398217.failure_event: The Hydra3_Component shall set the DTC if any one of the following criteria is met: 1. Open circuit/Short to battery failure if IN3 = L , Fault = L for 500 ms OR 2. Short to ground failure if IN3 = H, Fault = L for 500 ms Note : Depending upon the status of INx and Fault line, chip can read the failure. Refer to Hydra3 schematic diagram and HSIS documenttaion for the meaning of INx and Fault pins. SoC is going to determine the state of the Pins using its Video - In schematic block connection. For Rear camera, x = 3. So Channel 3 from CAM_CS_A is selected i.e. CAM3_PWR_EN is enabled for rear camera. OR 3. Thermal shutdown to occur if temperature reading is more than 125 deg C for 500 ms
6398217.possible_causes: - Views will be blanked: Rear view, Rear cross path view, Free floating view - Features that rely on Rear Camera will be unavailable
6398217.impact: - Views will be blanked: Rear view, Rear cross path view, Free floating view - Features that rely on Rear Camera will be unavailable
6398217.limp: - Output Video: Rear Video feed grayed out in all Views (Rear view, Rear cross path view, Free floating view) - Features Not available: i. Automatic Emergency Braking - Premium Rear Automatic Emergency Braking: RAEB ii. Full Automatic Park Assist System (APA) iii. Personal Park Assist iv. Remote Parking Assist
6398217.steps: 1. Check Harness 2. Ensure correct camera functionality 3. Restart the system Note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).
6398217.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6398217.note: The Register Address of the temperature sensor is R0x3E9A (Refer to developer's guide for AR0233AT Image Sensor: Section - Temperature Sensor) OR 4. Lock failure on deserializer side for 500 ms. OR 5. Not receiving any image, images are frozen, images are not being refreshed for 500ms OR 6. CRC Error (Read thru register over I2C) OR 7. Calibration data is not configured/Alignment is Incorrect or not completed OR 8. Calibration data cannot be read from the flash
6398217.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Continue 100 msec Trigger Reset Age

1761688.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. 50ms after the controller is powered on 2. The low voltage power supply voltage is higher than 7V 3. The water outlet temperature sensor has no fault Continuous 20ms Trigger Reset Age The water outlet temperature is higher than 90 ! for 1s The water outlet temperature is lower than 85 ! for 1s 40

6578592.description: APB ASIC is in wrong SPI mode.
6578592.subsystem: Set fault, if APB ASIC SPI Mode Change is not successfully finished.
6578592.failure_event: APB ASIC is by default at STM SPI mode after Power on reset. The target SPI mode is Bosch SPI mode. In init task, a SPI mode change sequence is done before any SPI communication. And software checks whether the SPI mode is the target after the SPI mode change sequence. If the SPI mode is not the target, this failure will be set.
6578592.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6578592.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6578593.description: ASIC Init test failed during init task
6578593.subsystem: Defect in the APB ASIC, the SPI failure detection function in the ASIC does not work.
6578593.failure_event: uC sends some failure test frames (address error, parity error, clock error) and evaluate the responses. The tests pass if the APB ASIC could detect the failure and set corresponding failure flags.
6578593.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6578593.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6578594.description: SPI transfer to APB ASIC was failed (error frame, wrong address. timeout, CSIH error)

6578594.failure_event: Investigate the receive data of the APB ASIC for parity-, address- or clock-failures. Time out failure is also included in this failure word.
6578594.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6578594.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6578597.description: APB Asic internal voltage bits stuck
6578597.subsystem: An internal voltage monitoring raised a failure. 1. Overvoltage, indicated by Vint_O_fail_SPI. 2. Undervoltage, indicated by Vint_U_fail_SPI. 3. Ground pins are not shorted internal in the APB ASIC, indicated by GNDloss_fail_SPI.
6578597.failure_event: Control of the internal voltage monitoring of the APB ASIC. If overvoltage, undervoltage or groundloss is detected in the APB Asic the failure counter get raised. Continuously activation in 5ms task. The monitoring threshold is 30ms.
6578597.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6578597.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6578598.description: Init test for APB Asic internal oscillator monitoring failed.
6578598.subsystem: The oscillator monitoring init test detect a malfunction or a stuck OSC_fail_SPI bit. The oscillator monitoring can be tested with a dedicated SPI command SPI_OSCtest = "1". The test will generate a stucked CLK1 and the monitoring has to switch the logic clock to CLK2. The failure has to be shown by OSC_fail_SPI.
6578598.failure_event: The oscillator monitoring init test detect a malfunction or a stuck bit inside the APB Asic. Triggers a failure at oscillator 1 and controls that the APB Asic performs as anticipated.
6578598.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6578598.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6398473.subsystem: 1. Harness defect 2. Camera defect 3. Camera is not powered as specified by ECU
6398473.failure_event: The Hydra3_Component shall set the DTC if any one of the following criteria is met: 1. Open circuit/Short to battery failure if IN4 = L , Fault = L for 500 ms OR 2. Short to ground failure if IN4 = H, Fault = L for 500 ms Note : Depending upon the status of INx and Fault line, chip can read the failure. Refer to Hydra3 schematic diagram and HSIS documenttaion for the meaning of INx and Fault pins. SoC is going to determine the state of the Pins using its 'Video - In' schematic block connection. For Front camera, x = 4. So Channel 4 from CAM_CS_A is selected i.e. CAM4_PWR_EN is enabled for Front camera. OR 3. Thermal shutdown occurs if temperature reading is more than 125 deg C for 500 ms
6398473.possible_causes: - Views will be blanked: Front view, Front cross path view, Free Floating view - Features that rely on Front Camera will be unavailable
6398473.impact: - Views will be blanked: Front view, Front cross path view, Free Floating view - Features that rely on Front Camera will be unavailable
6398473.limp: - Output Video: Front Video feed grayed out in all Views (Front view, Front cross path view, Free Floating view) - Features Not available: i. Full Automatic Park Assist System (APA) ii. Personal Park Assist iii. Remote Parking Assist
6398473.steps: 1. Check Harness 2. Ensure correct camera functionality 3. Restart the system Note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).
6398473.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6398473.note: The Register Address of the temperature sensor is R0x3E9A (Refer to developer's guide for AR0233AT Image Sensor: Section - Temperature Sensor) OR 4. Lock failure on deserializer side for 500 ms. OR 5. Not receiving any image, images are frozen, images are not being refreshed for 500ms OR 6. CRC Error (Read thru register over I2C) OR 7. Calibration data is not configured/Alignment is Incorrect or not completed OR 8. Calibration data cannot be read from the flash
6398473.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Continue 100 msec Trigger Reset Age

6398729.subsystem: 1. Harness defect 2. Camera defect 3. Camera is not powered as specified by ECU
6398729.failure_event: The Hydra3_Component shall set the DTC if any one of the following criteria is met: 1. Open circuit/Short to battery failure if IN2 = L , Fault = L for 500 ms OR 2. Short to ground failure if IN2 = H, Fault = L for 500 ms Note : Depending upon the status of INx and Fault line, chip can read the failure. Refer to Hydra3 schematic diagram and HSIS documenttaion for the meaning of INx and Fault pins. SoC is going to determine the state of the Pins using its 'Video - In' schematic block connection. For Left side camera, x = 2. So Channel 2 from CAM_CS_A is selected i.e. CAM2_PWR_EN is enabled for Left side camera. OR 3. Thermal shutdown occurs if temperature reading is more than 125 deg C for 500 ms
6398729.possible_causes: - Views will be blanked: Left side videos, Left Side split (curb) view and Left flank 3D view, free floating view - Features that rely on Left Camera will be unavailable
6398729.impact: - Views will be blanked: Left side videos, Left Side split (curb) view and Left flank 3D view, free floating view - Features that rely on Left Camera will be unavailable
6398729.limp: - Output Video: Left camera video feed grayed out in all Views (Curb view, left flank 3D view, free floating view) - Features Not available: i. Full Automatic Park Assist System (APA) ii. Personal Park Assist iii. Remote Parking Assist
6398729.steps: TBD Note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).
6398729.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6398729.note: The Register Address of the temperature sensor is R0x3E9A (Refer to developer's guide for AR0233AT Image Sensor: Section - Temperature Sensor) OR 4. Lock failure on deserializer side for 500 ms. OR 5. Not receiving any image, images are frozen, images are not being refreshed for 500ms OR 6. CRC Error (Read thru register over I2C) OR 7. Calibration data is not configured/Alignment is Incorrect or not completed OR 8. Calibration data cannot be read from the flash
6398729.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Continue 100 msec Trigger Reset Age

6398985.subsystem: 1. Harness defect 2. Camera defect 3. Camera is not powered as specified by ECU
6398985.failure_event: The Hydra3_Component shall set the DTC if any one of the following criteria is met: 1. Open circuit/Short to battery failure if IN1 = L , Fault = L for 500 ms OR 2. Short to ground failure if IN1 = H, Fault = L for 500 ms Note : Depending upon the status of INx and Fault line, chip can read the failure. Refer to Hydra3 schematic diagram and HSIS documenttaion for the meaning of INx and Fault pins. SoC is going to determine the state of the Pins using its 'Video - In' schematic block connection. For Right side camera, x = 1. So Channel 1 from CAM_CS_A is selected i.e. CAM1_PWR_EN is enabled for Right side camera. OR 3. Thermal shutdown occurs if temperature reading is more than 125 deg C for 500 ms
6398985.possible_causes: - Views will be blanked: Right side videos, Right Side split (curb) view and Right flank 3D view, free floating view - Features that rely on Right Camera will be unavailable
6398985.impact: - Views will be blanked: Right side videos, Right Side split (curb) view and Right flank 3D view, free floating view - Features that rely on Right Camera will be unavailable
6398985.limp: - Output Video: Right camera video feed grayed out in all Views (Curb view, Right flank 3D view, free floating view) - Features Not available: i. Full Automatic Park Assist System (APA) ii. Personal Park Assist iii. Remote Parking Assist
6398985.steps: TBD Note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).
6398985.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6398985.note: The Register Address of the temperature sensor is R0x3E9A (Refer to developer's guide for AR0233AT Image Sensor: Section - Temperature Sensor) OR 4. Lock failure on deserializer side for 500 ms. OR 5. Not receiving any image, images are frozen, images are not being refreshed for 500ms OR 6. CRC Error (Read thru register over I2C) OR 7. Calibration data is not configured/Alignment is Incorrect or not completed OR 8. Calibration data cannot be read from the flash
6398985.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Continue 100 msec Trigger Reset Age

10593536.subsystem: This DTC is detonated if the locking feedback received via digital input have a mismatch with the intended activation of the door locking motors.
10593536.failure_event: The feedback of driver door lock will be compared against the microcontroller pin activation. If no activation request is set by microncontroller and the feedback received is relay activated, an stuck on ON is considered. 6399497.subsystem: 1. Harness defect 2. Camera defect 3. Camera is not powered as specified by ECU
6399497.failure_event: The Hydra3_Component shall set the DTC if any one of the following criteria is met: 1. Open circuit/Short to battery failure if IN1 = L (CAM_BANK_B), Fault = L for 500 ms OR 2. Short to ground failure if IN1 = H (CAM_BANK_B), Fault = L for 500 ms Note : Depending upon the status of INx and Fault line, chip can read the failure. Refer to Hydra3 schematic diagram and HSIS documenttaion for the meaning of INx and Fault pins. SoC is going to determine the state of the Pins using its 'Video - In' schematic block connection. For DMS camera, x = 1 and Channel 5 from CAM_CS_B is selected i.e. CAM5_PWR_EN is enabled for DMS camera. OR The Hydra3_component shall log the DTC if it receives any one of the following Flags from DMS camera (OV2311) and the flag is present for 500 ms : 1. status OR 2. fail_safe OR 3. temperature OR 4. frame_counter OR 5. i2C_communication

6580131.description: RFP pump motor test failed. No generated motor voltage detected.
6580131.failure_event: The PumpMotorTest is an initial test, which will be triggered if the vehicle speed is higher than 38kph.The test detects a not turning motor (stalled motor) while the motor is actuated.
6580131.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6580131.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6580133.subsystem: Interruption, short circuit or defect component within freewheeling path.
6580133.failure_event: The FreewheelingPathTest is a cyclic test (every 20s outside motor actuation) to check, if Freewheeling (FW) FET and the ReverseVoltageProtection (RVP) FET is switchable. Additionally an interrupted motor, interrupted pin connections and interruptions in the complete comparator circuit and FWD path are detected.
6580133.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6580133.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6580134.subsystem: Defect of motor relay or gate driver.
6580134.failure_event: The MrgTest is a cyclic test (every 20s outside motor actuation) to check, if the motor actuation logic within ASIC works and if the motor relay is switchable.
6580134.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6580134.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6580135.description: ASIC internal problem in the Load Dump logic.
6580135.subsystem: Asic internal failure in load dump detection logic part.
6580135.failure_event: The LoadDumpTest is a cyclic test (every 20s outside motor actuation) to check, if the motor relay is actuated in case of a load dump.
6580135.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6580135.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6580136.description: ASIC internal logic problem (missing failure detection).
6580136.subsystem: Several Asic bits show unexpected behaviour after actuation / MrgTest in hydraulic off state.
6580136.failure_event: The InverseActTest (consists of InverseMrgTest and InverseMotorActuation) runs only once initially per power cycle (in hydraulic off state).The goal of the test is to check the functionality of the MrgTest and the MRDS ASIC bits.
6580136.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6580136.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6580137.subsystem: Interruption or short circuit within the complete UMgen measurement path. Especially interruptions or short circuits of the volrage divider pulldown resistor.
6580137.failure_event: The PassiveAdcConnectTest is a cyclic test (every 20s outside motor actuation) to check the complete UMGen measurement path. Especially interruptions or short circuits of the voltage divider pulldown resistor.
6580137.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6580137.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6580138.description: ASIC internal logic problem in the ANS/CCC modulator.
6580138.subsystem: Failure inside ASIC internal actuation logic.

14213168.cause: No Hall Sensor signal detected while track motor is moving.
14213168.impact: Automatic seat adjustment not working. Manual seat adjustment is only working in emergency mode.
14213168.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the regular CAN messages.
14213168.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connect the motor to DC voltage supply and check if the halls sensor is delivering a signal, while the motor is turning. In case that a hall sensor signal can't be measured, although the motor is turning, the motor has to be exchanged.
14213168.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age No Hall Sensor signal detected while track motor is moving. Hall sensor signals measured while track motor is moving. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

1141017.description: OBC DC side fast overcurrent fault(SW)
1141017.subsystem: 1. The HV load has shorted. 2. OBC has internal shorted. 3. The HV current sensor is abnormal. or 1. The AC load has shorted. 2. OBC has internal shorted. 3. The HV current sensor is abnormal.
1141017.failure_event: 1.Three phase charge command:|OBC_IDCSEC_1_PHY| > 44A, 300us; 2.Single phase charge command:|OBC_IDCSEC_1_PHY| > 31A, 300us; or 1.Discharge command:|OBC_IDCSEC_1_PHY| >22A, 600us
1141017.possible_causes: Record the fault and OBC stops immediately

1141017.impact: Record the fault and OBC stops immediately
1141017.steps: 1. Check whether the HV load is short-circuited and the AC load is short-circuited.

RADAR_INFO (0x300)||OBJ_DATA_00 (0x310)||OBJ_DATA_01 (0x311)||OBJ_DATA_02 (0x312)||OBJ_DATA_03 (0x313)||OBJ_DATA_04 (0x314)||OBJ_DATA_05 (0x315)||OBJ_DATA_06 (0x316)||OBJ_DATA_07 (0x317)||OBJ_DATA_08 (0x318)||OBJ_DATA_09 (0x319)||OBJ_DATA_10 (0x31A)||OBJ_DATA_11 (0x31B)||OBJ_DATA_12 (0x31C)||OBJ_DATA_13 (0x31D)||OBJ_DATA_14 (0x31E)||OBJ_DATA_15 (0x31F)||OBJ_DATA_16 (0x320)||OBJ_DATA_17 (0x321)||OBJ_DATA_18 (0x322)||OBJ_DATA_19 (0x323)||OBJ_DATA_20 (0x324)||OBJ_DATA_21 (0x325)||OBJ_DATA_22 (0x326)||OBJ_DATA_23 (0x327)||OBJ_DATA_24 (0x328)||OBJ_DATA_25 (0x329)||OBJ_DATA_26 (0x32A)||OBJ_DATA_27 (0x32B)||OBJ_DATA_28 (0x32C)||OBJ_DATA_29 (0x32D)||OBJ_DATA_30 (0x32E)||OBJ_DATA_31 (0x32F)||ROAD_BORDER (0x500)||RADAR_STATE (0x301)||RADAR_CFG (0x600) message missing for 200 msec 4. For the message RADAR_INFO (0x300)||OBJ_DATA_00 (0x310)||OBJ_DATA_01 (0x311)||OBJ_DATA_02 (0x312)||OBJ_DATA_03 (0x313)||OBJ_DATA_04 (0x314)||OBJ_DATA_05 (0x315)||OBJ_DATA_06 (0x316)||OBJ_DATA_07 (0x317)||OBJ_DATA_08 (0x318)||OBJ_DATA_09 (0x319)||OBJ_DATA_10 (0x31A)||OBJ_DATA_11 (0x31B)||OBJ_DATA_12 (0x31C)||OBJ_DATA_13 (0x31D)||OBJ_DATA_14 (0x31E)||OBJ_DATA_15 (0x31F)||OBJ_DATA_16 (0x320)||OBJ_DATA_17 (0x321)||OBJ_DATA_18 (0x322)||OBJ_DATA_19 (0x323)||OBJ_DATA_20 (0x324)||OBJ_DATA_21 (0x325)||OBJ_DATA_22 (0x326)||OBJ_DATA_23 (0x327)||OBJ_DATA_24 (0x328)||OBJ_DATA_25 (0x329)||OBJ_DATA_26 (0x32A)||OBJ_DATA_27 (0x32B)||OBJ_DATA_28 (0x32C)||OBJ_DATA_29 (0x32D)||OBJ_DATA_30 (0x32E)||OBJ_DATA_31 (0x32F)||ROAD_BORDER (0x500)||RADAR_CFG (0x600) ||RADAR_STATE (0x301), if - CRC verification error occurs in the last 3 consecutive messages OR - Maximum difference of Counter value (MaxDeltaCounterInit) > 1 for 3 times continuously OR - Counter value is repeated in the last 3 consecutive messages OR - MAC verification error occurs in the last 3 consecutive messages OR 5. CMRR-FR role and scan config is incorrect during start up i.e. Hydra3 receives RadarRoleConfig != 0x3 (RadarRoleConfig_ROLE_CORNER_FR) from message RADAR_CFG (0x600) during Start up via HSCAN3. OR 6. Hydra3 receives MRRTimesyncStatus = 0x0 (MRRTimesyncStatus_TSYNC_NOT_SYNCED) || 0x3 ( MRRTimesyncStatus_TSYNC_SNA) from RADAR_INFO (0x300) message and it is valid for 650 msec. OR 7. Hydra3 receives SensorStatus - 0xC (SENSOR_ST_SAFETY_ERROR) from RADAR_STATE (0x301) message and it is valid for 200 msec

6581153.description: wss undervoltage has been detected for FL wheel
6581153.subsystem: If the System ASIC is equipped with the boost converter, the power supply of the WSS is always sufficient.ECU internal failure
6581153.failure_event: Detection of the undervoltage situation is done with the System ASIC internal hardware comparator called K7. This comparator monitors the voltage between the WSS power supply line and WSS signal line. The monitoring threshold can be configured for Threshold A0 to A15 to match the power supply thresholds of the used WSS type. If the System ASIC is equipped with the boost converter, the power supply of the WSS is always sufficient.So failure reason ECU internal failure.
6581153.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6581153.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

10595346.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG

6581409.description: wss undervoltage has been detected for FR wheel
6581409.subsystem: If the System ASIC is equipped with the boost converter, the power supply of the WSS is always sufficient.ECU internal failure
6581409.failure_event: Detection of the undervoltage situation is done with the System ASIC internal hardware comparator called K7. This comparator monitors the voltage between the WSS power supply line and WSS signal line. The monitoring threshold can be configured for Threshold A0 to A15 to match the power supply thresholds of the used WSS type. If the System ASIC is equipped with the boost converter, the power supply of the WSS is always sufficient.So failure reason ECU internal failure.
6581409.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6581409.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6401289.description: Radar Module-Rear Right Corner (CMRR-RR) - Component Failure
6401289.failure_event: The Hydra3_component shall log the DTC if any one of the following conditions is met - 1. RADAR_INFO (0x300)||OBJ_DATA_00 (0x310)||OBJ_DATA_01 (0x311)||OBJ_DATA_02 (0x312)||OBJ_DATA_03 (0x313)||OBJ_DATA_04 (0x314)||OBJ_DATA_05 (0x315)||OBJ_DATA_06 (0x316)||OBJ_DATA_07 (0x317)||OBJ_DATA_08 (0x318)||OBJ_DATA_09 (0x319)||OBJ_DATA_10 (0x31A)||OBJ_DATA_11 (0x31B)||OBJ_DATA_12 (0x31C)||OBJ_DATA_13 (0x31D)||OBJ_DATA_14 (0x31E)||OBJ_DATA_15 (0x31F)||OBJ_DATA_16 (0x320)||OBJ_DATA_17 (0x321)||OBJ_DATA_18 (0x322)||OBJ_DATA_19 (0x323)||OBJ_DATA_20 (0x324)||OBJ_DATA_21 (0x325)||OBJ_DATA_22 (0x326)||OBJ_DATA_23 (0x327)||OBJ_DATA_24 (0x328)||OBJ_DATA_25 (0x329)||OBJ_DATA_26 (0x32A)||OBJ_DATA_27 (0x32B)||OBJ_DATA_28 (0x32C)||OBJ_DATA_29 (0x32D)||OBJ_DATA_30 (0x32E)||OBJ_DATA_31 (0x32F)||ROAD_BORDER (0x500)||RADAR_STATE (0x301) || RADAR_CFG (0x600) message missing for 200 msec OR 3. Hydra3 receives SensorStatus - 0x7 (SENSOR_ST_EXTERNAL_ERROR) from RADAR_STATE (0x301) message and it is valid for 200 msec OR 4. For the message RADAR_INFO (0x300)||OBJ_DATA_00 (0x310)||OBJ_DATA_01 (0x311)||OBJ_DATA_02 (0x312)||OBJ_DATA_03 (0x313)||OBJ_DATA_04 (0x314)||OBJ_DATA_05 (0x315)||OBJ_DATA_06 (0x316)||OBJ_DATA_07 (0x317)||OBJ_DATA_08 (0x318)||OBJ_DATA_09 (0x319)||OBJ_DATA_10 (0x31A)||OBJ_DATA_11 (0x31B)||OBJ_DATA_12 (0x31C)||OBJ_DATA_13 (0x31D)||OBJ_DATA_14 (0x31E)||OBJ_DATA_15 (0x31F)||OBJ_DATA_16 (0x320)||OBJ_DATA_17 (0x321)||OBJ_DATA_18 (0x322)||OBJ_DATA_19 (0x323)||OBJ_DATA_20 (0x324)||OBJ_DATA_21 (0x325)||OBJ_DATA_22 (0x326)||OBJ_DATA_23 (0x327)||OBJ_DATA_24 (0x328)||OBJ_DATA_25 (0x329)||OBJ_DATA_26 (0x32A)||OBJ_DATA_27 (0x32B)||OBJ_DATA_28 (0x32C)||OBJ_DATA_29 (0x32D)||OBJ_DATA_30 (0x32E)||OBJ_DATA_31 (0x32F)||ROAD_BORDER (0x500)||RADAR_CFG (0x600)||RADAR_STATE (0x301), if - CRC verification error occurs in the last 3 consecutive messages OR - Maximum difference of Counter value (MaxDeltaCounterInit) > 1 for 3 times continuously OR - Counter value is repeated in the last 3 consecutive messages OR - MAC verification error occurs in the last 3 consecutive messages OR 5. CMRR-RR role and scan config is incorrect during start up i.e. Hydra3 receives RadarRoleConfig != 0x5 (RadarRoleConfig_ROLE_CORNER_RR) from message RADAR_CFG (0x600) during Start up via HSCAN5. OR 6. Hydra3 receives MRRTimesyncStatus = 0x0 (MRRTimesyncStatus_TSYNC_NOT_SYNCED) || 0x3 ( MRRTimesyncStatus_TSYNC_SNA) from RADAR_INFO (0x300) message and it is valid for 650 msec. OR
6401289.impact: Features that rely on CMRR-RR will stop functioning or degrade
6401289.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Continue 65ms Trigger Reset Age

6581665.description: wss undervoltage has been detected for RL wheel
6581665.subsystem: If the System ASIC is equipped with the boost converter, the power supply of the WSS is always sufficient.ECU internal failure
6581665.failure_event: Detection of the undervoltage situation is done with the System ASIC internal hardware comparator called K7. This comparator monitors the voltage between the WSS power supply line and WSS signal line. The monitoring threshold can be configured for Threshold A0 to A15 to match the power supply thresholds of the used WSS type. If the System ASIC is equipped with the boost converter, the power supply of the WSS is always sufficient.So failure reason ECU internal failure.
6581665.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6581665.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6581921.description: wss undervoltage has been detected for RL wheel
6581921.subsystem: If the System ASIC is equipped with the boost converter, the power supply of the WSS is always sufficient.ECU internal failure
6581921.failure_event: Detection of the undervoltage situation is done with the System ASIC internal hardware comparator called K7. This comparator monitors the voltage between the WSS power supply line and WSS signal line. The monitoring threshold can be configured for Threshold A0 to A15 to match the power supply thresholds of the used WSS type. If the System ASIC is equipped with the boost converter, the power supply of the WSS is always sufficient.So failure reason ECU internal failure.
6581921.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6581921.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

14220119.description: Right Mirror Motor Y - Output circuit current below threshold/open load

14220119.impact: Mirror is not moving on this axis.

Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (mirror must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is below the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14220119.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Current drawn by the mirror motor is below the minimum threshold, so open load detection gets triggered. Measured motor current is within the defined current range. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

6582688.description: Monitoring of wheel direction differences. Faulty wheel not assignable
6582688.subsystem: 1.) At least one wheel speed sensor is mounted in wrong direction. 2.) At least one wrong wheel speed sensor is mounted. 3.) At least one wheel speed sensor is wrongly connected.
6582688.failure_event: The fault is set if the detected rotation directions of two wheels are not the same and two wheels have no direction OR if the detected rotation directions of two wheels are opposite of the detected rotation directions of the other two wheels. The rotation direction can only be logged with directional WSS.

6582688.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6582689.description: Monitoring of wheel speed differences. Faulty wheel not assignable
6582689.subsystem: 1.) Wrong tyre size. 2.) Wrong pulse wheel toothcount. 3.) The WSS is not correctly mounted, so that no signal is induced in sensor element. 4.) The WSS is dislodged from bracket, so that no signal is induced in sensor element. 5.) The airgap between sensor and encoding wheel is too big to induce sufficient signal in sensor element. 6.) Malfunction of sensor internal logic. 7.) Tone wheel not mounted correctly or demagnetized. 8.) The WSS signal is disturbed by loose contact. 9.) The WSS signal is disturbed by open circuit in the WSS line.
6582689.failure_event: This failure is set if the difference between maximum and minimum wheel speed related to vehicle speed exceeds a calculated threshold. This threshold is depending on vehicle speed and current system mode. A generic fault is set, if the faulty wheel is not always the same. 1.) ESP Mode: For vehicle speeds lower than a specific value %{ 5.56 | m/s | 2 | SspWss_MonVdiff_v_Switch5 }% the failure threshold is an absolute value %{ 1.4 | m/s | 2 | SspWss_MonVdiff_vDiff_Lo5/SspWss_MonVdiff_v_SREF_perc }%. Above this vehicle speed the failure threshold is a relative value %{ 5 | % | 2 | SspWss_MonVdiff_vDiff_Lo5 }%. 2.) ABS and backup-EBD Mode: For vehicle speeds lower than a specific value %{ 27.8 | m/s | 2 | SspWss_MonVdiff_v_Switch6 }% the failure threshold is an absolute value %{ 1.7 | m/s | 2 | SspWss_MonVdiff_vDiff_Lo6/SspWss_MonVdiff_v_SREF_perc }%. Above this vehicle speed the failure threshold is a relative value %{ 6 | % | 2 | SspWss_MonVdiff_vDiff_Lo6 }%.
6582689.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6582689.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

6582696.subsystem: 1.) The WSS is not correctly mounted, so that no signal is induced in sensor element. 2.) The WSS is dislodged from bracket, so that no signal is induced in sensor element. 3.) The airgap between sensor and encoding wheel is too big to induce sufficient signal in sensor element. 4.) Malfunction of sensor internal logic. 5.) Tone wheel not mounted correctly or demagnetized. 6.) The Input amplifier in ECU is faulty. 7.) The WSS signal is disturbed by loose contact. 8.) The WSS signal is disturbed by open circuit in the WSS line.
6582696.failure_event: The monitoring checks for a lost signal fault and consists of following parts: 1.) drive off low monitoring: The failure is set after a particular wheel speed stays at zero for a minimum time %{ 0.5 | s | 1 | }% after drive off and afterwards the vehicle velocity increases by a specific threshold %{ 3.4 | m/s | 1 | }%. The monitoring is able to detect up to two defect wheel speed sensors.2.) drive off high monitoring: The failure is set after a particular wheel speed drops to zero and the vehicle velocity increases by a specific threshold %{ 5 | m/s | 2 | MAX(MAX((SspWss_vDOLO_CheckFailLower*((48/2)/(AnzahlZaehne_VA/Abrollumfang_VA))),SspWss_vDOLO_Check FailLower),SspWss_v_DOHI_Offset)}%. The monitoring is able to detect up to two defect wheel speed sensors.3.) dynamic monitoring: The failure is set after a particular wheel speed drops to zero and no edges of the encoding wheel are delivered by the sensor.From functional perspective, the monitoring shall not be curable within the same ignition cycle, although in DEM it is configured as curable. This is only implemented like this to be able to use DEM functionality "Sequential failure buffer" to synchronize with HSW WSS monitors. The monitor will never reset in the same cycle after a fault is detected without HSW fault detected.
6582696.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6582696.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).

The airgap between sensor and encoding wheel is too big to induce sufficient signal in sensor element. 4.) Malfunction of sensor internal logic. 5.) Tone wheel not mounted correctly or demagnetized. 6.) The Input amplifier in ECU is faulty. 7.) The WSS signal is disturbed by loose contact. 8.) The WSS signal is disturbed by open circuit in the WSS line.
6582701.failure_event: The monitoring checks for a lost signal fault and consists of following parts: 1.) drive off low monitoring: The failure is set after a particular wheel speed stays at zero for a minimum time %{ 0.5 | s | 1 | }% after drive off and afterwards the vehicle velocity increases by a specific threshold %{ 3.4 | m/s | 1 | }%. The monitoring is able to detect up to two defect wheel speed sensors.2.) drive off high monitoring: The failure is set after a particular wheel speed drops to zero and the vehicle velocity increases by a specific threshold %{ 5 | m/s | 2 | MAX(MAX((SspWss_vDOLO_CheckFailLower*((48/2)/(AnzahlZaehne_VA/Abrollumfang_VA))),SspWss_vDOLO_Check FailLower),SspWss_v_DOHI_Offset)}%. The monitoring is able to detect up to two defect wheel speed sensors.3.) dynamic monitoring: The failure is set after a particular wheel speed drops to zero and no edges of the encoding wheel are delivered by the sensor.From functional perspective, the monitoring shall not be curable within the same ignition cycle, although in DEM it is configured as curable. This is only implemented like this to be able to use DEM functionality "Sequential failure buffer" to synchronize with HSW WSS monitors. The monitor will never reset in the same cycle after a fault is detected without HSW fault detected.

6402560.description: ADAS DC Secure Protocol Data Unit (PDU) Authentication Fail
6402560.failure_event: The Hydra3_component shall set the DTC If - Freshness value (FV) synchronization message verification fails OR - Abnormal freshness value synchronization message is received.
6402560.possible_causes: FV synchronization message will be dropped
6402560.impact: FV synchronization message will be dropped
6402560.limp: For High-Speed Safety features: FID_AEB_Critical_Impact FID_VRU_Critical_Impact FID_SCP_Critical_Impact FID_RCTAB_Critical_Impact FID_LTAP_Critical_Impact FID_ESA_Critical_Impact FID_LKA_Critical_Impact For the definition of above limp-home actions, refer to Requirement 16315106 Features that are in error state/Not available/ Deactivated: All the comfort features and low speed safety features will be unavailable. Comfort and low speed safety Feature List - 1. Lane Change Assist (LCA) 2. Traffic jam assist (TJA) 3. Integrated Cruise Assist (ICA) 4. Automatic Cruise Control (ACC) Including Stop and Go 5. Auto High Beam (AHB) 6. Intelligent Speed Assist (ISA) 7. Blind Spot Monitoring (BSM) 8. Traffic Sign/Light Recognition (TSLR) 9.
6402560.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
6402560.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).
6402560.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V) && BCM_PwrMod == IGN ON(0x2)|| RUN(0x4) && BUS physical status && De- bounce timer Continuous Failure to authenticate at any time (Need to update the rate based on secured pdu verification time) Trigger Reset Age

14184997.description: Seat Length Adjustment Motor - Circuit current below threshold/open load
14184997.cause: Current is below the minimum threshold for more than 500ms.
14184997.impact: Seat can't be moved on this axis. 0xE00326;Seat Backrest Adjustment Motor - Circuit current below threshold/open load;DTCU187326;U187326;0;Service_Relevant;"Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness.
14184997.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (seat must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is below the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14184997.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age The current drawn by the motor is below the minimum threshold of tbd V. This indicates a broken cable motor. Current drawn by the motor is above the minimum of V. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14185511.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. Check for scrubbed free cables. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (seat must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is above the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14185511.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age The current drawn by the motor is above the maximum threshold of tbd V. This indicates a broken cable motor. Current drawn by the motor is below the maximum threshold of V. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14186025.description: Seat Backrest Adjustment Motor - Circuit current above threshold/short to GND
14186025.cause: Current is above the maximum threshold for more than 500ms.
14186025.impact: Seat can't be moved on this axis. Risk of thermal event and destruction of the electrical circuitry.
14186025.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the
14186025.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. Check for scrubbed free cables. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (seat must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is above the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14186025.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age The current drawn by the motor is above the maximum threshold of tbd V. This indicates a broken cable motor. Current drawn by the motor is below the maximum threshold of V. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14186288.cause: No Hall Sensor signal detected while track motor is moving.
14186288.impact: Automatic seat adjustment not working. Manual seat adjustment is only working in emergency mode.
14186288.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the regular CAN messages.
14186288.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connect the motor to DC voltage supply and check if the halls sensor is delivering a signal, while the motor is turning. In case that a hall sensor signal can't be measured, although the motor is turning, the motor has to be exchanged.

Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14186545.cause: No Hall Sensor signal detected while backrest motor is moving.
14186545.impact: Automatic seat adjustment not working. Manual seat adjustment is only working in emergency mode.
14186545.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the regular CAN messages.
14186545.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connect the motor to DC voltage supply and check if the halls sensor is delivering a signal, while the motor is turning. In case that a hall sensor signal can't be measured, although the motor is turning, the motor has to be exchanged.
14186545.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age No Hall Sensor signal detected while backrest motor is moving. Hall sensor signals measured while backrest motor is moving. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14219348.description: Left Mirror Motor X - Output circuit current below threshold/Open Load
14219348.cause: Current drawn by the mirror motor is below the minimum threshold, so open load detection gets triggered.
14219348.impact: Mirror is not moving on this axis.
14219348.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the regular CAN messages.
14219348.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (mirror must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is below the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14219348.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Current drawn by the mirror motor is below the minimum threshold, so open load detection gets triggered. Measured motor current is within the defined current range. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.
