14186802.cause: No Hall Sensor signal detected while height motor is moving.
14186802.impact: Automatic seat adjustment not working. Manual seat adjustment is only working in emergency mode.
14186802.limp: Record DTC. Failure will be reported over the regular CAN messages.
14186802.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connect the motor to DC voltage supply and check if the halls sensor is delivering a signal, while the motor is turning. In case that a hall sensor signal can't be measured, although the motor is turning, the motor has to be exchanged.

14219862.description: Right Mirror Motor X - Output circuit current below threshold/open load
14219862.cause: Current drawn by the mirror motor is below the minimum threshold, so open load detection gets triggered.
14219862.impact: Mirror is not moving on this axis.
14219862.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (mirror must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is below the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14219862.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Current drawn by the mirror motor is below the minimum threshold, so open load detection gets triggered. Measured motor current is within the defined current range. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

10599189.description: SBR_4 2nd row right seat short to BATT or Open
10599189.subsystem: This DTC is detonated if the resistance in the input is higher than threshold define to this signal, during a determined time.
10599189.failure_event: The input resistance will be checked to monitor the failure, if the resistance is higher than specific power threshold(based on HW), it's considered as failure.
10599189.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
10599189.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).
10599189.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate Voltage supply is in the normal range(9V~16v) continuous 40ms Trigger Reset Age The input resistance will be checked to monitor the failure, if the resistance is higher than specific power t hreshold(based on HW), it's considered as failure. The input resistnace will be checked to monitor the failure, if the resistance is lower than specific power t hreshold(based on HW), it's considered as No failure. After 100 IGN ON to OFF cycles,clear history DTC automatically

14220376.description: Left Mirror Motor X - Output circuit current above threshold/short to GND
14220376.cause: Overcurrent detection was triggered, by a current above the maximum threshold.
14220376.impact: Mirror is not moving on this axis.
14220376.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (mirror must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is above the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14220376.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Overcurrent detection was triggered, by a current above the maximum threshold. Overcurrent detection wasn't triggered. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14220896.description: Right Mirror Motor X - Output circuit current above threshold/short to GND
14220896.cause: Overcurrent detection was triggered, by a current above the maximum threshold.
14220896.impact: Mirror is not moving on this axis.
14220896.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (mirror must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is above the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14220896.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Overcurrent detection was triggered, by a current above the maximum threshold. Overcurrent detection wasn't triggered. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

14221153.cause: Overcurrent detection was triggered, by a current above the maximum threshold.
14221153.impact: Mirror is not moving on this axis.
14221153.repair: Visual inspection of connector on motor and ECU side. Check cable that connects motor and ECU by measung resistance. In case that damaged replace connector or wiring harness. Connector motor to DC voltage supply and check the current drawn be the motor (mirror must be moving). In case that motor draws a current that is above the current specified in the datasheet replace the motor.
14221153.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. Local voltage is within normal range (9-16V)/ Ignition ON or ECU is awake 2. De-bounce timer = 5 sec Event 10 ms Trigger Reset Age Overcurrent detection was triggered, by a current above the maximum threshold. Overcurrent detection wasn't triggered. Failure 10 ignition cycles long not recognized.

1699862.description: Battery Voltage Failure - voltage is below threshold
1699862.subsystem: Vehicle battery voltage is too low.
1699862.failure_event: Battery Voltage < 9 V and last for 2 sec
1699862.steps: - Ensure stable power supply to ADAS DC module. - Voltage returns to 8.5 -16.5 V. (Considering +/- 0.5V tolerance from the normal voltage range 9-16 V) - Reconnect the battery/power supply
1699862.reference: Reference Connector End View Information N/A Reference Electrical Information Basic Methods of Circuit Inspection & refer CAN Diagnosis TSG
1699862.note: Ensure the customer has not installed any unauthorized accessories (e.g., Speakers, Power Horns, Headlights, etc.).
1699862.trigger: Monitor when Monitor type Monitor rate 1. ACC ON 2. De-bounce timer = 2 sec Continuous 100 ms Trigger Reset Age Battery Voltage < 9 V and last for 2 sec Battery Voltage >9 V for 2 sec 40 warmup cycles
